project.name: gaal
project.description: Governed Agent Access Layer — a Go CLI (Go 1.26+) that reconciles AI agent setups across machines from a single YAML file, syncing repositories (git/hg/svn/bzr/tar/zip), installing SKILL.md skill collections into agent directories (Claude Code, Copilot, Cursor, Codex), and upserting MCP server entries into agent JSON configs. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.summary: Reports what would be (or was) imported during a migrate run: target agent, repositories, skills, MCPs, and dry-run flag.

nodes.name: version.go
nodes.summary: Cobra subcommand that prints the gaal CLI version (and build time) in text or JSON form; also registers the user-agent string with the httpx client at init.
nodes.languageNotes: Version and BuildTime are intended to be injected via -ldflags -X at build time.

nodes.name: manager.go
nodes.summary: Implements the generic content sync engine: resolves source roots (local paths or shallow-cloned remote repos), copies declared paths into per-agent skill roots with atomic directory replacement, and records snapshots for drift detection.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses tmp-dir + os.Rename for atomic directory replacement to avoid partially-written targets on crash.

nodes.name: Manager
nodes.summary: Holds the user's content entries plus runtime context (cache dir, home, workspace, state dir, force flag) and exposes Sync/Status methods that iterate every declared content target.

nodes.name: Status
nodes.summary: Per-target status record returned by Manager.Status: identifies source/agent/scope/root and aggregates per-path PathStatus entries plus an optional error.

nodes.name: PathStatus
nodes.summary: Per-path status snapshot used to render the content rows of the status report: source/target paths plus Present, Dirty, and Error flags.

nodes.name: NewManager
nodes.summary: Constructs a Manager from the parsed content entries and the runtime directories the sync needs (cache, home, workdir, state).

nodes.name: syncOne
nodes.summary: Resolves a single content source to a local root, then copies each (source -> dest) mapping into every resolved agent root, recording a snapshot after each copy.

nodes.name: statusOne
nodes.summary: Builds Status records for one content entry by walking its targets, resolving agent roots, and stat-ing each declared destination to compute Present/Dirty flags.

nodes.name: resolveSource
nodes.summary: Returns a local root for a content source: an expanded local path for filesystem sources, or a shallow-cloned cache dir (clone-on-first-use, best-effort update afterwards) for remote sources.

nodes.name: copyDirReplace
nodes.summary: Atomically replaces dst by staging into a sibling tmp dir, removing the old dst, then renaming the tmp into place so a crash never leaves a half-written target.

nodes.name: copyFileAtomic
nodes.summary: Writes src to a temp file in dst's parent directory, chmods to a sanitized mode (0o755 if any-exec, else 0o644), then renames the temp file into place.

nodes.name: dirModified
nodes.summary: Drift detector that walks both src and dst, comparing every non-VCS regular file byte-for-byte and flagging any file present only on one side.

nodes.name: writeContentSnapshot
nodes.summary: Persists a discover.Snapshot for dest under a content-prefixed key in the state dir so subsequent drift checks have a baseline to diff against.

nodes.name: archive.go
nodes.summary: VCS backend for tar/zip archives fetched over HTTP(S): downloads with body-size caps, then safely extracts with per-entry size caps, entry-count caps, symlink rejection, and path-containment checks against zip-slip.
nodes.languageNotes: All extractors enforce three resource caps (body, per-file, entry-count) and reject non-regular entries to defend against archive bombs and traversal attacks.

nodes.name: VcsArchive
nodes.summary: VCS implementation for tar/zip archive sources; Format selects 'tar' or 'zip' and the version field is reused as an optional sub-directory prefix to strip on extraction.

nodes.name: extractTar
nodes.summary: Streams a tar (auto-detects gzip non-destructively via peeked magic bytes), enforces entry count and per-file size caps, and rejects symlinks/hardlinks/devices to avoid the symlink-overwrite attack.

nodes.name: extractZip
nodes.summary: Buffers the zip body to a tempfile (zip needs ReaderAt), enforces overall and entry-count size caps, skips non-regular entries, and writes each file via writeFileLimited.

nodes.name: archiveEntryPath
nodes.summary: Converts an archive entry name to a contained destination path, rejecting absolute paths and parent-traversal entries and enforcing a final filepath.Rel containment check against dest.

nodes.name: writeFileLimited
nodes.summary: Copies up to maxBytes+1 from r into path so a single oversize entry cannot fill the disk; returns errEntryTooLarge when the cap is exceeded.

nodes.name: fetchURL
nodes.summary: Performs the HTTP GET that backs archive cloning: validates the URL with urlx, builds the request via httpx (loopback/internal blocked), and returns the body for the caller to stream.

nodes.name: archive_test.go
nodes.summary: Exercises VcsArchive end-to-end with synthetic tar.gz and zip blobs served by httptest, plus targeted tests for the size caps, symlink rejection, and path-containment guards.

nodes.name: bzr.go
nodes.summary: VCS backend that shells out to the bzr binary for clone (`bzr branch`), update (`bzr pull --overwrite`), version (`bzr revno`), and dirty-check (`bzr status -S`).

nodes.name: VcsBazaar
nodes.summary: Empty struct that satisfies the VCS interface for Bazaar repositories by delegating every operation to the bzr binary.

nodes.name: bzr_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests VcsBazaar by injecting a fake bzr binary at the front of PATH, covering both missing-binary errors and the parsed output of `bzr revno`/`bzr status`.

nodes.name: destination.go
nodes.summary: Provides CheckEmptyDestination, the pre-clone guard that refuses to write into a non-empty directory; centralises the refusal policy so every VCS backend shares it (issue #217).

nodes.name: NonEmptyDestinationError
nodes.summary: Typed error carrying the destination path and a (possibly truncated) list of entries found there; its Error message tells the user how to resolve the conflict.

nodes.name: CheckEmptyDestination
nodes.summary: Returns nil when path is absent or empty, a plain error when it exists and is not a directory, and a *NonEmptyDestinationError listing the offending entries otherwise.

nodes.name: destination_test.go
nodes.summary: Covers CheckEmptyDestination across missing/empty/non-empty/file destinations, plus the NonEmptyDestinationError message format and entry truncation logic.

nodes.name: detect.go
nodes.summary: Heuristic VCS-type inference: inspects local directories for .git/.hg/.svn/.bzr markers and falls back to URL-suffix detection (.tar.gz/.zip) before defaulting to git.

nodes.name: DetectType
nodes.summary: Public entrypoint that routes local paths to directory-marker detection and remote URLs to suffix-based detection.

nodes.name: isLocalFS
nodes.summary: Recognises POSIX absolute, Windows drive-letter absolute, relative, and ~-home paths so DetectType can branch into the local-vs-remote split (issue #231).

nodes.name: detect_test.go
nodes.summary: Table-driven coverage of DetectType across remote git/archive URLs and every local VCS marker, plus the NewShallow git-only specialisation.

nodes.name: git.go
nodes.summary: VCS backend implemented on top of go-git: clones (optionally shallow), fetches/pulls, resolves a version pin against local branch, remote branch, tag, then commit hash, and reports current version via tag/branch/short-hash.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure-Go via go-git; no git binary required. Shallow mode hard-resets to origin/HEAD instead of pulling so force-pushes/history rewrites are mirrored.

nodes.name: VcsGit
nodes.summary: Git VCS backend struct; the single Shallow field switches between full clone+pull and depth-1 clone with hard-reset semantics suitable for skill caches.

nodes.name: Clone
nodes.summary: Validates the URL, ensures the parent dir exists, then invokes go-git's PlainCloneContext with recursive submodules and all tags; optionally checks out version after.

nodes.name: Update
nodes.summary: Opens the local repo, returns a *RemoteURLMismatchError when the configured URL disagrees with origin, fetches with prune+force, then either checks out version, hard-resets to origin (shallow), or pulls.

nodes.name: checkoutVersion
nodes.summary: Resolves version against local branch first, then remote-tracking branch (creating a local branch), then tag, then literal commit hash, returning an error when none match.

nodes.name: tagAtCommit
nodes.summary: Iterates all tags (lightweight and annotated) and returns the short name of the first one pointing at hash, breaking the iteration with the errTagFound sentinel.

nodes.name: resetToRemoteHEAD
nodes.summary: Resolves origin's default branch via remote-tracking HEAD/main/master, then hard-resets the working tree so shallow caches mirror force-pushes and history rewrites.

nodes.name: git_mismatch.go
nodes.summary: Defines RemoteURLMismatchError plus URL-normalisation helpers used by VcsGit.Update to surface protocol/host disagreements between gaal.yaml and the working copy's origin.

nodes.name: RemoteURLMismatchError
nodes.summary: Typed error returned when gaal.yaml's URL and the working copy's origin URL disagree; its message labels both protocols and suggests the exact `git remote set-url` fix.

nodes.name: normalizeGitURL
nodes.summary: Canonicalises a git URL to host+path by stripping scheme, credentials, trailing .git, and trailing slashes (host lower-cased, path case preserved) so equivalent URLs compare equal.

nodes.name: urlProtocolLabel
nodes.summary: Returns a human-readable protocol label (HTTPS, SSH, file://, …) used inside the mismatch error message so users can immediately see which protocols disagree.

nodes.name: isSCPStyleGitURL
nodes.summary: Detects user@host:path URLs (scp-style, which url.Parse won't recognise) so normalisation can route them through the SSH branch.

nodes.name: git_mismatch_test.go
nodes.summary: Table-driven tests for normalizeGitURL and urlProtocolLabel plus assertions on the RemoteURLMismatchError message format and errors.As behaviour.

nodes.name: git_test.go
nodes.summary: Comprehensive VcsGit coverage built on local bare repos: clone with version/tag, fetch+update, tag-vs-branch CurrentVersion, HasChanges clean/modified, shallow reset semantics, and URL-mismatch checks.

nodes.name: hg.go
nodes.summary: VCS backend that shells out to the hg binary: clone via `hg clone -r`, update via `hg pull` + `hg update -r`, version via `hg id -i`, and dirty-check via `hg status -mard`.

nodes.name: VcsMercurial
nodes.summary: Empty struct satisfying the VCS interface for Mercurial by delegating every operation to the hg binary.

nodes.name: hg_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests VcsMercurial with a fake hg binary at the front of PATH, covering missing-binary errors and the parsed outputs of `hg id`/`hg status`.

nodes.name: svn.go
nodes.summary: VCS backend that shells out to svn for checkout/update and to svnversion for the current revision; uses `svn status --depth infinity -q` for dirty detection.

nodes.name: VcsSVN
nodes.summary: Empty struct satisfying the VCS interface for Subversion by delegating to the svn and svnversion binaries.

nodes.name: svn_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests VcsSVN with fake svn/svnversion binaries on PATH, asserting both the missing-binary error path and parsed status output.

nodes.name: testutil_test.go
nodes.summary: Shared test helper makeFakeBin that drops a stub executable at the front of PATH so VCS backend tests can simulate subprocess responses deterministically.

nodes.name: util.go
nodes.summary: VCS package-private helpers: requireBinary error wrapper, cmdOutput for read-only subprocess queries, shortPath for log-friendly display, and validateVCSOperand against argv flag injection.
nodes.languageNotes: validateVCSOperand defends against attacks like `version: '--config=hooks.preupdate=touch /tmp/pwned'`.

nodes.name: util_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the package-private util.go helpers: operand validation, requireBinary lookup, shortPath formatting, and cmdOutput success/failure/cancellation paths.

nodes.name: vcs.go
nodes.summary: Defines the VCS interface (Clone/Update/IsCloned/CurrentVersion/HasChanges) and the New/NewShallow factories that select the right backend by type, plus compile-time assertions wiring every implementor.
nodes.languageNotes: The `var _ VCS = (*VcsGit)(nil)` block makes the compiler enforce that every backend keeps the interface satisfied.

nodes.name: VCS
nodes.summary: Common VCS interface all backends implement: Clone/Update with URL+version+path, IsCloned predicate, CurrentVersion description, and HasChanges dirty detection.

nodes.name: New
nodes.summary: Factory that returns the VCS implementation for vcsType ('git'/'hg'/'svn'/'bzr'/'tar'/'zip') or an error for unsupported types.

nodes.name: NewShallow
nodes.summary: Like New, but returns a depth-1 VcsGit when the type is 'git' (suitable for skill caches that never need history); falls through to New for other types.

nodes.name: vcs_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests the New/NewShallow factories: each known type maps to the right backend struct, and the archive entry preserves its Format field.

nodes.name: global.go
nodes.summary: Scans every agent's canonical and extended skill directories (workspace + global + package-manager roots) and returns Resource records, attributing each SKILL.md directory exactly once across passes and computing drift via VCS-native or snapshot-based diffs.
nodes.languageNotes: Two-pass strategy: canonical install dirs first, extended search lists second, with a `seen` set to keep each skill attributed to its primary agent.

nodes.name: scanGlobal
nodes.summary: Walks every registered agent's project, global, and package-manager skill roots, dedup'ing across passes via a shared seen-set so a skill is attributed to its canonical owner first.

nodes.name: skillsFromDir
nodes.summary: Returns a Resource for each immediate subdirectory containing SKILL.md (and dir itself when it is a skill root), computing drift and attributing each to agentName.

nodes.name: computeSkillDrift
nodes.summary: Returns a DriftState for a skill directory: prefers VCS-native dirty detection when a marker exists, falling back to snapshot-vs-disk diff under stateDir.

nodes.name: parseSkillFrontmatter
nodes.summary: Reads name/description from a SKILL.md's YAML frontmatter via yaml.v3 (issue #133); tolerates CRLF, quoted values and colons, returning empty strings on any failure so callers fall back to the directory base name.

nodes.name: walkSkillDirs
nodes.summary: Recursively finds every directory literally named 'skills' under root and returns them; used to locate package-manager-installed skill roots without descending into their contents.

nodes.name: global_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for the global skill scanner: frontmatter parsing happy/edge cases, VCS-marker detection, skillsFromDir dedup, and computeSkillDrift across ok/modified states.

nodes.name: mcp_test.go
nodes.summary: Exercises computeMCPDrift across the snapshot-baseline-missing, ok, modified, and missing-file states using a temp state-dir and a stub MCP config file.

nodes.name: installed.go
nodes.summary: Tiny helper exporting IsAgentInstalled that reports whether the named agent has any skills directory present on disk at either scope.

nodes.name: IsAgentInstalled
nodes.summary: Reports whether the named agent appears installed by stat'ing its global and project skill directories.

nodes.name: installed_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests covering the IsAgentInstalled predicate at project scope, global scope, neither, and unknown-agent edge cases.

nodes.name: scan.go
nodes.summary: Parses SKILL.md frontmatter and provides ScanDir / WalkForSkillDirs helpers that locate skill directories one level deep (or recursively for package-manager roots).

nodes.name: Meta
nodes.summary: Parsed shape of a SKILL.md file's YAML frontmatter (name, description, source dir, etc.).

nodes.name: ParseSkillMeta
nodes.summary: Reads a SKILL.md file, extracts its YAML frontmatter, and falls back to the parent directory name when no name field is declared.

nodes.name: extractFrontmatter
nodes.summary: Returns the YAML block between the first two `---` delimiters of a markdown file, or empty when none is present.

nodes.name: ScanDir
nodes.summary: Lists immediate child directories of root that contain a SKILL.md file, also handling the special case where root itself is a skill.

nodes.name: WalkForSkillDirs
nodes.summary: Recursively walks a package-manager install root looking for sub-trees that own a `skills/` folder, stopping descent once one is found.

nodes.name: scan_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for ParseSkillMeta, ScanDir, and WalkForSkillDirs covering frontmatter parsing, fallback to directory name, root-as-skill detection, and non-recursion semantics.

nodes.name: mkSkill
nodes.summary: Test helper that creates a directory with a minimal SKILL.md file containing the given frontmatter name.

nodes.name: TestParseSkillMetaExported_WithFrontmatter
nodes.summary: Confirms ParseSkillMeta returns the name field from valid frontmatter.

nodes.name: TestParseSkillMetaExported_NoName_FallbackToDirName
nodes.summary: Frontmatter without a name field must fall back to the parent directory name.

nodes.name: TestScanDir_SubdirsWithSkillMD
nodes.summary: ScanDir returns each immediate subdirectory that owns a SKILL.md file.

nodes.name: TestScanDir_RootIsASkill
nodes.summary: When root itself is a skill (root/SKILL.md), ScanDir returns the root path rather than descending.

nodes.name: TestScanDir_NoRecursion
nodes.summary: ScanDir must not recurse below one level even when grand-children own SKILL.md files.

nodes.name: TestWalkForSkillDirs_FindsSkillsDirs
nodes.summary: WalkForSkillDirs returns every nested directory that owns a `skills/` subdirectory inside the search root.

nodes.name: TestWalkForSkillDirs_NoDescendIntoSkillsSubdirs
nodes.summary: Once a directory containing a `skills/` folder is found, WalkForSkillDirs must stop descending into it.

nodes.name: telemetry.go
nodes.summary: Package-level telemetry orchestration: resolves consent state, initialises an HTTP client, tracks events with categorised errors and base properties, and persists one-shot milestones (install/first_sync) to a secfile-protected JSON.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses sync.WaitGroup to drain pending HTTP requests on Shutdown so the binary doesn't exit before events flush.

nodes.name: milestoneState
nodes.summary: Persistent flag struct tracking which one-shot telemetry events (install, first-sync) have already been sent.

nodes.name: Init
nodes.summary: Resolves opt-in/opt-out consent (prompting the user if needed), initialises the HTTP client and base props, optionally deferring persistence until FlushConsent runs.

nodes.name: Shutdown
nodes.summary: Drains any in-flight tracking requests with a bounded timeout so the process can exit cleanly without losing events.

nodes.name: FlushConsent
nodes.summary: Persists a deferred consent decision recorded by Init when deferPersist is true, writing it to the user config file.

nodes.name: Track
nodes.summary: Sends a non-custom telemetry event with the standard property bag attached, no-op when telemetry is disabled.

nodes.name: TrackCustom
nodes.summary: Sends a telemetry event with caller-provided custom properties merged on top of the base props.

nodes.name: TrackError
nodes.summary: Tracks a categorised error event by bucketing the error message into one of a fixed set of categories.

nodes.name: TrackFirstSync
nodes.summary: Emits the first_sync milestone exactly once across runs by consulting and updating the persisted milestoneState.

nodes.name: categorizeError
nodes.summary: Maps a raw error message to one of the canonical telemetry buckets (network, auth, fs, schema, ...) so cardinality stays bounded.

nodes.name: installedAgentNames
nodes.summary: Returns a sorted, deduped list of agent names currently detected on disk for inclusion as a telemetry property.

nodes.name: loadMilestoneState
nodes.summary: Reads the on-disk milestoneState JSON, returning a zero-valued struct when the file is missing or unreadable.

nodes.name: saveMilestoneState
nodes.summary: Atomically writes the milestoneState JSON to disk via secfile.Write so the file is created with 0o600.

nodes.name: info.go
nodes.summary: Implements `gaal info` rendering for the four package types (repo, skill, mcp, agent) in JSON, table (pterm), and text output formats with substring filtering.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses pterm for richly formatted CLI tables and a manual ANSI-aware width measurement helper.

nodes.name: Info
nodes.summary: Public entry that dispatches to the right renderer based on requested package type and output format, after collecting a full status report.

nodes.name: renderInfoJSON
nodes.summary: Serialises only the section of the status report matching the requested package type (and filter) as indented JSON to the writer.

nodes.name: infoBoxes
nodes.summary: Renders a set of titled boxes side-by-side using pterm, padded to a uniform width and grouped into the terminal's available columns.

nodes.name: renderRepoInfo
nodes.summary: Renders the repository section as a pterm table with declared path, source, branch, and managed status, applying the filter substring.

nodes.name: renderSkillInfo
nodes.summary: Renders the skills section in table form including per-agent install counts and a nested name tree for skills sharing a source.

nodes.name: renderAgentInfo
nodes.summary: Renders the agents section: per-agent overview with detected install status, skill count, and scope-specific MCP config paths.

nodes.name: renderMCPInfo
nodes.summary: Renders the MCP servers section with per-entry source kind, scope, and the resolved target files for each consuming agent.

nodes.name: codec.go
nodes.summary: Pluggable codec layer that reads and writes the MCP-servers table for each agent's config file format, supporting JSON (default) and Codex-style TOML while preserving unrelated keys.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses an interface-with-private-method pattern to keep the codec contract internal to the package.

nodes.name: mcpCodec
nodes.summary: Interface abstracting per-format read/write of the MCP servers table inside an agent's config file.

nodes.name: jsonCodec
nodes.summary: JSON implementation of mcpCodec used by Claude Desktop, VS Code, Cursor, and any agent whose MCP config is JSON.

nodes.name: tomlCodec
nodes.summary: TOML implementation of mcpCodec used by Codex's config.toml, preserving sibling keys (model, sandbox, [analytics]) across upserts.

nodes.name: codecFor
nodes.summary: Selects the appropriate codec based on the target file's extension, defaulting to JSON.

nodes.name: readOrderedJSON
nodes.summary: Reads a JSON document while preserving its top-level key ordering so writeOrderedJSON can round-trip it without reshuffling the user's file.

nodes.name: writeOrderedJSON
nodes.summary: Writes a JSON document while preserving the recorded top-level key ordering returned by readOrderedJSON.

nodes.name: decodeTOMLEntry
nodes.summary: Converts a TOML mcp_servers entry into the internal serverEntry shape, mapping command, args, env, headers, and URL.

nodes.name: encodeTOMLEntry
nodes.summary: Serialises an internal serverEntry back into TOML using sorted key ordering for deterministic diffs.

nodes.name: codec_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the codec dispatch and TOML round-trip behaviour, asserting unrelated keys, env mappings, and HTTP headers survive upserts.

nodes.name: TestTOMLCodec_RoundTripPreservesUnrelatedKeys
nodes.summary: Loads a config.toml with model/sandbox/analytics keys, upserts an MCP entry, and verifies every unrelated key remains intact and in order.

nodes.name: TestTOMLCodec_UpsertReplacesExistingEntry
nodes.summary: Writing an existing server key with new fields replaces the entry in-place rather than appending a duplicate.

nodes.name: TestTOMLCodec_PreservesHTTPHeaderEnvMappings
nodes.summary: Round-trips HTTP MCP entries with header env-mapping tables and asserts the structure survives serialisation without flattening.

nodes.name: TestCodecFor_DispatchesByExtension
nodes.summary: Confirms codecFor returns the TOML codec for .toml paths and JSON otherwise.

nodes.name: doctor.go
nodes.summary: Implements the `gaal doctor` operation: runs schema, telemetry, skill source, MCP target, agent and tool checks against the loaded config and returns a structured DoctorReport with exit-code semantics.

nodes.name: Finding
nodes.summary: Single doctor check result with section, severity (info/warning/error), and human-readable message.

nodes.name: DoctorReport
nodes.summary: Structured output of the doctor pipeline: list of findings, per-config-level summary, and computed exit code.

nodes.name: DoctorOptions
nodes.summary: Configures the doctor run: offline mode, the unmerged config levels, and the workspace directory used for project-scope detection.

nodes.name: RunDoctor
nodes.summary: Top-level orchestrator that executes every doctor check in sequence and aggregates findings into a DoctorReport with an exit code (0/1/2).

nodes.name: buildConfigLevels
nodes.summary: Produces a per-level ConfigLevelSummary (global, user, workspace) showing which files loaded and how many repos/skills/MCPs each contributes.

nodes.name: checkSchema
nodes.summary: Validates the loaded config's schema version against the current binary's expectation and emits findings for mismatches or missing versions.

nodes.name: checkSkillSources
nodes.summary: Verifies every declared skill source resolves on disk (local) or is reachable (remote), respecting the offline flag and reporting errors per source.

nodes.name: checkMCPTargets
nodes.summary: For every MCP entry confirms the target file's parent directory exists and the agent supports the requested scope (global/project).

nodes.name: checkAgents
nodes.summary: Counts how many declared agents are actually installed on disk and emits an informational finding with the tally.

nodes.name: checkRemoteReachable
nodes.summary: Issues a HEAD/GET probe against a remote skill source URL with a short timeout to detect unreachable hosts in non-offline mode.

nodes.name: checkTools
nodes.summary: Walks every tool declared in the config, invokes the tools.Check probe, and emits a finding per missing or version-mismatched binary.

nodes.name: logger.go
nodes.summary: Initialises the global slog logger with a tee handler that writes both to stderr (level-filtered) and an append-only on-disk log file with tight permissions.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements the slog.Handler interface (Enabled/Handle/WithAttrs/WithGroup) to multiplex output to two sinks.

nodes.name: teeHandler
nodes.summary: slog.Handler implementation that forwards every record to two wrapped handlers (stderr + file), returning Enabled if either accepts the level.

nodes.name: Setup
nodes.summary: Initialises the package's default slog logger from a verbosity flag and an optional state-dir file path, wiring up the tee handler.

nodes.name: agents.go
nodes.summary: Thin facade re-exporting the agent registry's name list, lookup, SkillDir, and MCP path helpers so the skill package can avoid importing core/agent directly in every call site.

nodes.name: AgentNames
nodes.summary: Returns the sorted list of registered agent names by delegating to the core agent registry.

nodes.name: SkillDir
nodes.summary: Resolves the on-disk skills directory for an (agent, scope) tuple via the core agent registry.

nodes.name: Lookup
nodes.summary: Re-exports agent.Lookup so callers in the skill package can resolve an agent's Info struct without importing core/agent.

nodes.name: ProjectMCPConfigPath
nodes.summary: Re-exports the agent registry's ProjectMCPConfigPath helper.

nodes.name: agents_test.go
nodes.summary: Smoke tests asserting the agents-facade re-exports return non-empty values and behave consistently with the underlying registry on POSIX and Windows.

nodes.name: TestExpandHome_POSIX
nodes.summary: Verifies the tilde-expansion helper substitutes the HOME env value on POSIX systems.

nodes.name: registry.go
nodes.summary: Loads the embedded agents.yaml plus any user override file into an in-memory registry, exposing lookups (Names, List, Lookup) and per-agent skill/MCP path resolvers used across the codebase.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses //go:embed agents.yaml and a package-level init() that merges built-in entries with ~/.config/gaal/agents.yaml.

nodes.name: Info
nodes.summary: Describes a coding agent's filesystem layout (skill dirs, MCP config files, search paths, platform support) consumed by sync and audit operations.

nodes.name: init
nodes.summary: Package init hook that decodes the embedded agents.yaml into the registry map and merges the optional user override file.

nodes.name: loadInto
nodes.summary: Strictly decodes a YAML agents file into the target map, applying override semantics and rejecting duplicate keys unless allowOverride is set.

nodes.name: validateEntry
nodes.summary: Enforces invariants on agent entries: tilde prefix for home-anchored paths, no absolute or dot-dot project paths, and recognised supported_platforms values.

nodes.name: Names
nodes.summary: Returns the sorted list of agent names known to the registry.

nodes.name: List
nodes.summary: Returns the registry's contents as a sorted slice of Entry (name + Info), suitable for iteration in deterministic order.

nodes.name: Lookup
nodes.summary: Returns the Info value for a named agent and whether it was found.

nodes.name: SkillDir
nodes.summary: Resolves the on-disk skills directory for an (agent, scope) tuple, transparently redirecting agents that share the generic .agents/skills convention.

nodes.name: GlobalMCPConfigPath
nodes.summary: Expands and returns the user-global MCP config file path for the named agent, when one is declared.

nodes.name: ProjectMCPConfigPath
nodes.summary: Expands and returns the workspace-scoped MCP config file path for the named agent, when one is declared.

nodes.name: ExpandedProjectSkillsSearch
nodes.summary: Expands the project-relative skills search paths for the named agent into ready-to-use absolute paths.

nodes.name: ExpandedGlobalSkillsSearch
nodes.summary: Expands the home-anchored global skills search paths for the named agent into absolute filesystem paths.

nodes.name: ExpandedPmSkillsSearch
nodes.summary: Expands the package-manager-installed skill search roots (e.g. ~/.claude/plugins) for the named agent.

nodes.name: ExpandHome
nodes.summary: Resolves a tilde-prefixed path to its absolute form using os.UserHomeDir.

nodes.name: registry_internal_test.go
nodes.summary: White-box tests that exercise the unexported loadInto and validateEntry helpers, covering duplicate-key handling, override allowance, and every path-shape invariant.

nodes.name: TestLoadInto_ValidYAML
nodes.summary: Smoke test for the happy path: a well-formed agents.yaml decodes into the expected entries.

nodes.name: TestLoadInto_Duplicate_NoOverride_ReturnsError
nodes.summary: Confirms loadInto refuses to silently overwrite an existing agent when allowOverride is false.

nodes.name: TestValidateEntry_GlobalDirWithoutTilde_Rejected
nodes.summary: Rejects global skill dir values that fail to anchor with a tilde (~/) prefix.

nodes.name: TestLoadInto_UserFile_CannotOverrideBuiltin
nodes.summary: Verifies users cannot redefine a built-in agent through their personal agents.yaml override file.

nodes.name: TestUserAgentsPath_DarwinUsesXDGConfigHome
nodes.summary: Asserts the user agents override path honours XDG_CONFIG_HOME on Darwin.

nodes.name: registry_test.go
nodes.summary: Black-box tests covering the agent registry's public surface: Names, Lookup, List, SkillDir, MCPConfigPath, and the generic-agent redirection rules.

nodes.name: TestNames_NonEmpty

nodes.name: TestAllAgents_HaveNonEmptySkillDirs

nodes.name: TestGenericAgentRegistered
nodes.summary: Confirms the synthetic `generic` agent that owns .agents/skills is always present in the registry.

nodes.name: secfile.go
nodes.summary: Tiny utility package for writing and opening secret-bearing files with 0o600 mode, tightening overly permissive existing files before write.

nodes.name: Write
nodes.summary: Atomically writes data to path with 0o600 mode, tightening existing files that are too permissive.

nodes.name: OpenAppend
nodes.summary: Opens a file for append-only writes with 0o600 mode, retroactively tightening the existing file's permissions when needed.

nodes.name: secfile_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests confirming Write and OpenAppend create files at 0o600 and tighten any loose permissions already present.

nodes.name: TestWrite_NewFileIs0o600
nodes.summary: New file written by Write must land on disk with mode exactly 0o600.

nodes.name: TestWrite_TightensExistingLooseFile
nodes.summary: Existing file with mode 0o644 must have its permissions tightened to 0o600 on the next Write.

nodes.name: TestOpenAppend_TightensExistingLooseFile
nodes.summary: OpenAppend must tighten an existing world-readable file to 0o600 before returning its handle.

nodes.name: mcp.go
nodes.summary: Discovers existing MCP configuration files on disk by querying every registered agent's project/global config paths, regardless of any gaal.yaml.

nodes.name: scanMCPs
nodes.summary: Walks every registered agent and returns Resource entries for any MCP config files present on disk, covering both workspace and global scopes.

nodes.name: appendMCPResource
nodes.summary: Stats an MCP config file and appends a Resource entry when present and not yet seen, deduping across agents that share paths.

nodes.name: computeMCPDrift
nodes.summary: Compares the SHA-256 of an MCP config against a snapshot record, using stat metadata as a fast path before falling back to a full hash.

nodes.name: audit.go
nodes.summary: Top-level Audit operation that scans every agent's project, global, and PM skill directories plus MCP configs, deduplicates ownership, and renders the result.

nodes.name: Audit
nodes.summary: Public entry point that collects discovered skills and MCPs and pipes them through the AuditRenderer for the requested output format.

nodes.name: collectAuditSkills
nodes.summary: Two-pass scan over every agent's canonical and search skill directories that attributes shared directories to the canonical owner before adding fallback owners.

nodes.name: collectAuditMCPs
nodes.summary: Iterates every agent's project and global MCP config files and aggregates them into AuditMCPEntry rows with content parsed via mcp.ListServers.

nodes.name: scanDeduped
nodes.summary: Helper that scans a directory for SKILL.md files via skill.ScanDir while skipping any path already attributed to another agent.

nodes.name: darwin.go
nodes.summary: Darwin-specific user config-directory resolver that honors XDG_CONFIG_HOME before falling back to ~/Library/Application Support semantics overridden by the package's exported GlobalConfigFilePath.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Go build constraint //go:build darwin so only compiles on macOS.

nodes.name: GlobalConfigFilePath
nodes.summary: Returns the macOS system-wide gaal config file path under /etc/gaal/gaal.yaml.

nodes.name: userConfigDir
nodes.summary: Resolves the per-user config directory honoring XDG_CONFIG_HOME first, otherwise falling back to ~/Library/Application Support/gaal on macOS.

nodes.name: manager.go
nodes.summary: Cross-platform entry points that expose UserConfigFilePath and UserConfigDir to callers, delegating per-OS lookups to the platform-specific build-tagged files.

nodes.name: UserConfigFilePath
nodes.summary: Public wrapper that returns the resolved per-user gaal.yaml path for the current OS.

nodes.name: UserConfigDir
nodes.summary: Public wrapper that returns the per-user gaal configuration directory across platforms.

nodes.name: manager_test.go
nodes.summary: Cross-platform unit tests that exercise UserConfigFilePath, UserConfigDir, and GlobalConfigFilePath with XDG, AppData, and HOME fallback scenarios.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses t.Skip with runtime.GOOS checks to guard OS-specific test bodies.

nodes.name: TestUserConfigFilePath_FallbackWhenHomeBroken
nodes.summary: Validates that userConfigFilePath does not panic when HOME is unset and falls back gracefully.

nodes.name: TestUserConfigDir_DarwinNoXDGFallsBackToHome
nodes.summary: Verifies Darwin path resolution falls back to ~/Library/Application Support/gaal when XDG_CONFIG_HOME is unset.

nodes.name: unix.go
nodes.summary: Linux/Unix platform implementation that returns /etc/gaal/gaal.yaml for global and defers to os.UserConfigDir for per-user resolution.

nodes.name: windows.go
nodes.summary: Windows platform implementation that resolves global config via PROGRAMDATA and the per-user dir under APPDATA via os.UserConfigDir.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses //go:build windows tag and os.Getenv("PROGRAMDATA") with a hardcoded C:/ProgramData fallback.

nodes.name: GlobalConfigFilePath
nodes.summary: Resolves the system-wide gaal.yaml path on Windows using PROGRAMDATA with a C:/ProgramData fallback.

nodes.name: behavior.go
nodes.summary: Defines per-agent capability flags (skills, MCP global/project, platform support) and emits scoped Warning values when configurations target unsupported agent behaviours.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a Scope/WarningCode enum pair so callers dedupe by (Code, Agent) across wildcard expansions.

nodes.name: Behavior
nodes.summary: Struct describing what a registered agent can do (skills, MCP global/project, platforms) plus a Validate method that returns scope-filtered warnings.

nodes.name: Warning
nodes.summary: Data shape emitted by Behavior.Validate with WarningCode, agent name, scope, message, and remediation hint.

nodes.name: Validate
nodes.summary: Reports behavioural mismatches for an operation of the given Scope targeting this agent on the given goos, used to flag unsupported skill/MCP/platform combinations.

nodes.name: BehaviorFor
nodes.summary: Looks up a Behavior for the named agent from the registry, returning the zero value when the agent is unknown.

nodes.name: CollectWarnings
nodes.summary: Iterates a list of Group entries (agent×scope) and aggregates deduped warnings across wildcards and repeated scopes for surfacing to the user.

nodes.name: behavior_test.go
nodes.summary: Table-driven tests exercising Behavior.Validate across every (scope, support flag, goos) combination and verifying CollectWarnings dedupes and orders output stably.

nodes.name: TestBehaviorValidate_Matrix
nodes.summary: Matrix-style test that walks every combination of behavior support flags, scope, and goos to confirm the warning set is exactly the expected one for each cell.

nodes.name: TestBehaviorValidate_CombinesPlatformAndScopeWarnings
nodes.summary: Asserts platform and scope warnings stack together when an agent is unsupported on the host OS and is missing the requested capability.

nodes.name: TestBehaviorFor_ClaudeDesktop_HasYAMLOverrides
nodes.summary: Verifies the claude-desktop agent's YAML-declared overrides flow through into the resolved Behavior struct.

nodes.name: TestCollectWarnings_DedupesAcrossScopes
nodes.summary: Confirms identical (Code, Agent) warnings raised under both skill-global and skill-project scopes collapse to a single emitted line.

nodes.name: TestCollectWarnings_StableOrder
nodes.summary: Guarantees CollectWarnings returns deterministically ordered output regardless of input group order.

nodes.name: resource.go
nodes.summary: Defines the generic Resource value type plus the ResourceType, Scope, and DriftState string-enum constants that the discover package uses to describe filesystem-found skills, repos, and MCP configs.

nodes.name: Resource
nodes.summary: Generic, config-independent struct describing a discovered managed resource (skill directory, VCS repo, or MCP config file) including its type, scope, drift state, and free-form metadata.

nodes.name: resource_test.go
nodes.summary: Pins the string values of ResourceType, Scope, and DriftState constants so external consumers and JSON serialization formats do not silently break.

nodes.name: scan.go
nodes.summary: Entry point for FS-first resource discovery: orchestrates a timeout-bounded scan over global agent paths, MCP config files, and the workspace tree, returning a deduplicated Resource list with drift state enriched from the snapshot index.

nodes.name: ScanOptions
nodes.summary: Options struct controlling discover.Scan: max walk depth, workspace inclusion toggle, state directory for drift detection, and overall timeout.

nodes.name: Scan
nodes.summary: Top-level scan function: applies defaults, runs global, MCP, and (optionally) workspace scans under a shared deadline, deduplicates by absolute path, and returns the merged Resource slice.

nodes.name: scan_test.go
nodes.summary: Verifies that discover.Scan honours its options (depth, workspace flag, timeout) and that the deduplication and drift enrichment paths behave correctly on synthesised filesystems.

nodes.name: redact_test.go
nodes.summary: Verifies the redacting slog.Handler removes credentials from top-level, grouped, and WithAttrs-attached string values across many URL shapes.

nodes.name: manager.go
nodes.summary: MCP server-config manager: resolves agent-fan-out, fetches remote entries via hardened HTTP, merges them into per-agent JSON config files, writes post-sync snapshots, and reports drift.

nodes.name: Manager
nodes.summary: Coordinates synchronisation, pruning, and status reporting of MCP server entries across all configured agent targets.

nodes.name: Status
nodes.summary: Per-MCP-entry status record holding the target path, presence flag, drift indicator, and any sync error.

nodes.name: NewManager
nodes.summary: Constructs an MCP Manager bound to the configured entries, the user's home directory (for ~/ expansion), and the gaal state directory.

nodes.name: resolvedMCPs
nodes.summary: Expands each ConfigMcp into concrete per-agent entries using the registry, supporting wildcard fan-out while keeping backwards compatibility with explicit Target overrides.

nodes.name: Sync
nodes.summary: Top-level sync entry point that iterates every resolved MCP entry and delegates to syncOne, accumulating errors.

nodes.name: syncOne
nodes.summary: Synchronises a single MCP entry: fetches the remote server JSON when URL-backed, merges it into the agent's config file, and writes a snapshot of the result.

nodes.name: fetchRemoteEntry
nodes.summary: Validates a remote URL via urlx.ValidateRemoteFetchURL, fetches the body with the hardened httpx client under a body-size cap, and decodes it into a serverEntry.

nodes.name: mergeIntoTarget
nodes.summary: Reads the agent's existing MCP servers JSON document, merges the new server entry in by name, and atomically writes the file back preserving unrelated keys.

nodes.name: Prune
nodes.summary: Removes MCP entries from each agent config file that are no longer declared in the gaal config, comparing against the per-target snapshot to leave external entries untouched.

nodes.name: Status
nodes.summary: Reports per-entry presence and drift for every configured MCP entry by reading the current target file and comparing against the configured/inline definition.

nodes.name: LoadServers
nodes.summary: Reads an MCP servers JSON file from path and returns the parsed serverEntry map, preserving any extra top-level keys for round-tripping.

nodes.name: ListServers
nodes.summary: Returns the sorted list of MCP server names declared in the given target file — used by inspection commands and tests.

nodes.name: backends_test.go
nodes.summary: Cross-VCS-backend integration tests for repo.Manager, covering git, hg, svn, bzr, and archive sources to ensure each backend correctly clones, updates, and reports status.

nodes.name: manager.go
nodes.summary: Manages parallel sync of all configured repositories, dispatching to the appropriate VCS backend per entry and aggregating per-repo Status records for the engine.

nodes.name: Status
nodes.summary: Per-repository sync state — path, type, URL, configured/current version, cloned flag, dirty flag, and any error.

nodes.name: Manager
nodes.summary: Repository sync manager that fan-out clones/updates every entry from the config map in parallel.

nodes.name: NewManager
nodes.summary: Constructs a repo Manager bound to the configured repo map and the gaal state directory.

nodes.name: Sync
nodes.summary: Clones or updates every configured repository in parallel using a wait group and error channel; the first error per repo is surfaced.

nodes.name: syncOne
nodes.summary: Validates the URL with urlx.ValidateRepoURL, picks the appropriate VCS backend, and clones or fetches into the configured destination path.

nodes.name: Status
nodes.summary: Walks each configured repo and returns its current Status: cloned flag, current vs configured version, and dirty (uncommitted changes) state.

nodes.name: run.go
nodes.summary: Subprocess runner that switches between streaming subprocess output as debug logs (DEBUG level) and showing a TTY spinner with quiet capture (INFO level), routing failure output through slog.Error.

nodes.name: Run
nodes.summary: Executes a command in a directory, picking runDebug or runInfo depending on whether slog DEBUG level is enabled.

nodes.name: runDebug
nodes.summary: Captures subprocess stdout+stderr into a buffer, runs to completion, and emits each non-empty line as slog.Debug — avoids pipe-synchronisation deadlocks.

nodes.name: runInfo
nodes.summary: Captures subprocess output silently and shows a TTY spinner with the supplied label; on failure dumps the captured output via slog.Error.

nodes.name: run_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests the runner across both verbosity branches, asserting that subprocess output is captured and surfaced correctly on success and failure.

nodes.name: manager.go
nodes.summary: Skill manager that resolves remote and local sources, scans SKILL.md files using agent-aware discovery paths, installs to per-agent target directories, fans out to the registered agent set, and writes/prunes post-sync snapshots.

nodes.name: SkillMeta
nodes.summary: Parsed SKILL.md frontmatter holding name, description, and absolute directory path of a discovered skill.

nodes.name: Status
nodes.summary: Per-skill-config status with source, agent, scope, and lists of installed, missing, and modified skill names.

nodes.name: Manager
nodes.summary: Skill installation manager that owns the local cache, working directory, gaal state root, and force-install behaviour.

nodes.name: buildDiscoveryDirs
nodes.summary: Builds the deduplicated list of subdirectories to scan for SKILL.md files inside a source repo, combining a generic set with every registered agent's skill directories.

nodes.name: NewManager
nodes.summary: Constructs a skill Manager with the configured entries, cache directory, home and working directories, state directory, and force-install flag.

nodes.name: SourcePaths
nodes.summary: Returns the absolute paths of every local source declared in the config — used by reconcileSkills to filter discovered skills that live under user-provided sources.

nodes.name: Sync
nodes.summary: Top-level skill sync entry point that iterates every configured skill entry and delegates to syncOne, accumulating errors.

nodes.name: syncOne
nodes.summary: Synchronises a single skill entry: resolves the source (local path or cached clone), discovers matching SKILL.md files, filters by name patterns, and installs into each target agent's skills directory.

nodes.name: resolveSource
nodes.summary: Resolves a skill source spec to a local directory, cloning or updating into the per-source cache when the spec is a URL and re-using local paths verbatim.

nodes.name: targetSkillsDir
nodes.summary: Computes the absolute target skills directory for a given agent and scope (global vs workspace), honouring the agent registry's path conventions.

nodes.name: Status
nodes.summary: Reports per-config Status, listing which skills are installed, which are missing, and which differ from their source after comparing on-disk files to the cache.

nodes.name: discoverSkills
nodes.summary: Walks the source directory across all discovery subdirectories and returns SkillMeta entries for every valid SKILL.md it finds.

nodes.name: filterSkills
nodes.summary: Filters discovered skills against the user-supplied glob patterns from the config, supporting wildcard selection.

nodes.name: installSkill
nodes.summary: Copies a discovered skill's directory tree into the target agent's skills directory, refusing to overwrite unmanaged content and creating directories as needed.

nodes.name: skillDirModified
nodes.summary: Detects whether an installed skill directory has been modified relative to its source by walking and comparing every file's content.

nodes.name: isLocalPath
nodes.summary: Reports whether a source spec refers to a local filesystem path (absolute, tilde, or `./`) as opposed to a remote URL.

nodes.name: toCloneURL
nodes.summary: Normalises a configured source spec into a clone URL accepted by the VCS backends.

nodes.name: urlToCacheKey
nodes.summary: Hashes a remote URL to a stable short identifier used as the per-source directory name inside the skill cache.

nodes.name: Prune
nodes.summary: Removes installed skills that are no longer declared in the gaal config, comparing the live filesystem against the per-target snapshot to leave unmanaged entries untouched.

nodes.name: writeSkillSnapshot
nodes.summary: Records a post-sync snapshot of the installed skill set so Prune can later identify the gaal-managed entries.

nodes.name: redact.go
nodes.summary: Strips embedded userinfo from URLs before they reach logs, errors, or telemetry — the canonical credential-redaction utility used throughout the codebase.

nodes.name: Redact
nodes.summary: Parses rawurl, strips u.User, and returns the rebuilt string; falls back to the input unchanged when parsing fails or no userinfo is present.

nodes.name: redact_test.go
nodes.summary: Exercises Redact across user-only, user-and-password, no-userinfo, and malformed URL inputs.

nodes.name: httpx.go
nodes.summary: Centralised hardened HTTP client for outbound fetches with TLS 1.2 minimum, a 10-hop redirect cap, scheme-downgrade and SSRF-redirect rejection, and a configurable stable User-Agent.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses sync/atomic.Value for lock-free User-Agent storage and a shared *http.Client so connection pooling carries across all fetches.

nodes.name: newClient
nodes.summary: Builds the singleton *http.Client with proxy-from-env, HTTP/2 attempt, idle pool limits, TLS 1.2 minimum, the global timeout, and the redirect-policy hook.

nodes.name: checkRedirect
nodes.summary: CheckRedirect callback that caps redirect chains, rejects https→http downgrades to non-loopback hosts, and blocks external→internal hops to defend against SSRF via redirect.

nodes.name: isLoopback
nodes.summary: Returns true when host:port literal resolves to a loopback address — used to allow http:// inside CI/e2e fixtures.

nodes.name: isInternal
nodes.summary: Reports whether host:port falls in loopback, link-local, or RFC1918/RFC4193 private space; central to the SSRF redirect guard.

nodes.name: httpx_test.go
nodes.summary: Drives the hardened client through redirect chains, scheme downgrades, and SSRF-redirect attempts to confirm each guard fires correctly.

nodes.name: console.go
nodes.summary: Custom slog.Handler producing compact, level-coloured single-line console output with TTY auto-detection and locked writes to coordinate with the progress spinner.

nodes.name: ConsoleHandler
nodes.summary: slog.Handler implementation that emits compact colourised log lines; carries pre-attached attributes and group prefix while serialising writes via a mutex.

nodes.name: NewConsoleHandler
nodes.summary: Constructs a ConsoleHandler bound to out, auto-detecting whether colour escapes should be emitted by checking that out is a TTY.

nodes.name: Handle
nodes.summary: slog.Handler.Handle implementation that formats the record with level-specific colouring, applies the group prefix, and writes the resulting line under the shared TTY mutex.

nodes.name: WithAttrs
nodes.summary: Returns a new ConsoleHandler with additional attributes pre-attached, copying internal state so the original handler remains untouched.

nodes.name: WithGroup
nodes.summary: Returns a new ConsoleHandler with an additional group-prefix segment appended.

nodes.name: writeAttr
nodes.summary: Formats a single slog.Attr (key=value) with dim-cyan key colour and dim-white value colour, recursing into group attrs.

nodes.name: fmtValue
nodes.summary: Renders a slog.Value as a string, quoting strings that contain spaces or special characters so the output stays parseable.

nodes.name: logger_test.go
nodes.summary: Comprehensive suite covering ConsoleHandler formatting, WithAttrs/WithGroup propagation, TTY colour detection, and progress spinner interaction.

nodes.name: progress.go
nodes.summary: TTY braille-style progress spinner with a single global active instance, guarded by the same mutex as the console handler so log lines never collide with the animated frame.

nodes.name: Spinner
nodes.summary: Animated TTY progress indicator with nil-safe methods; access to the drawn-state flag is serialised via the package-level ttyMu mutex.

nodes.name: StartSpinner
nodes.summary: Starts a new spinner on out, returning nil when out is not a TTY or another spinner is already active to prevent stacked animations.

nodes.name: Done
nodes.summary: Stops the spinner and writes a final check or cross glyph with the supplied label, leaving the terminal in a clean state.

nodes.name: Stop
nodes.summary: Halts the spinner without printing a result line — used when a higher-level component is about to write its own status.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Animation goroutine: ticks every 80ms, advances the frame index, and rewrites the spinner line under the TTY mutex until stop is signalled.

nodes.name: redact.go
nodes.summary: Defence-in-depth slog.Handler middleware that walks every string attribute (including those nested in groups) and runs urlx.Redact before forwarding to the inner handler.

nodes.name: redactingHandler
nodes.summary: Wrapping slog.Handler that strips credentials from string attribute values before forwarding the record to an inner handler.

nodes.name: redactAttr
nodes.summary: Recursively redacts string values inside an slog.Attr, descending into groups so credentials in nested URLs cannot leak.

nodes.name: scheme.go
nodes.summary: URL scheme allow-listing for outbound HTTP fetches and VCS clones; defends against file:// exfil, SSRF via internal-host http://, and unintended protocol transports.

nodes.name: ValidateRemoteFetchURL
nodes.summary: Rejects any URL gaal should not fetch over HTTP — only https:// any-host, and http:// to loopback for CI fixtures are allowed.

nodes.name: ValidateRepoURL
nodes.summary: Validates VCS clone URLs: allows https/ssh/git anywhere, gates http/svn/bzr to loopback, accepts SCP-style git URLs and Windows drive paths, and rejects file:// and exotic schemes.

nodes.name: isLoopbackHost
nodes.summary: Reports whether host:port is a loopback literal (localhost, 127.0.0.0/8, ::1) without performing DNS resolution.

nodes.name: isWindowsDrivePath
nodes.summary: Recognises C:\path / C:/path style Windows local paths that net/url misparses as scheme=c so they can be treated as local filesystem references.

nodes.name: isSCPStyleGitURL
nodes.summary: Detects user@host:path SCP-style git remote URLs, which net/url does not parse but git clone accepts directly.

nodes.name: scheme_test.go
nodes.summary: Drives ValidateRemoteFetchURL and ValidateRepoURL through allow-listed schemes, loopback edge cases, SCP-style git URLs, and rejected exotic schemes.

nodes.name: snapshot.go
nodes.summary: Implements the persistent post-sync snapshot index that records per-file size, mtime, and SHA-256 hash, plus DiffPath which compares a snapshot against the live filesystem to compute per-file drift events.
nodes.languageNotes: Mirrors Git's index-style fast-path: stat-only comparison short-circuits the expensive SHA-256 recompute.

nodes.name: FileRecord
nodes.summary: Per-file metadata record (size, mtime, SHA-256 hash) used as the fast-path drift-check unit; JSON-serialised inside snapshot files.

nodes.name: Change
nodes.summary: Single file-level drift event (path + DriftState) emitted by DiffPath when comparing a snapshot to the live filesystem.

nodes.name: Load
nodes.summary: Reads and decodes a JSON snapshot file, returning an empty (non-nil) Snapshot when the file is absent so first-run state is handled transparently.

nodes.name: Save
nodes.summary: Atomically writes a Snapshot to disk as JSON, creating parent directories as needed.

nodes.name: Record
nodes.summary: Builds a FileRecord from an on-disk file by stat-ing it and hashing its contents — used while populating a fresh post-sync snapshot.

nodes.name: SnapshotDir
nodes.summary: Walks a directory and produces a Snapshot mapping each contained file to a freshly computed FileRecord, used to capture the post-sync state of a skill or repo.

nodes.name: DiffPath
nodes.summary: Compares a snapshot key's recorded state against the live filesystem path, returning Change events for added, removed, and modified files using a stat-first fast path before falling back to content hashing.

nodes.name: hashFile
nodes.summary: Streams a file through SHA-256 and returns the 32-byte digest used as the content fingerprint inside FileRecord.

nodes.name: snapshot_test.go
nodes.summary: Exercises every snapshot path: load/save round-trips, mtime-only versus content drift detection, and edge cases like missing files and renamed entries.

nodes.name: workspace.go
nodes.summary: Depth-limited filesystem walk of the project workspace that identifies VCS repositories (via marker directories) and SKILL.md-bearing skill folders, returning them as Resource entries.

nodes.name: scanWorkspace
nodes.summary: Walks workDir up to maxDepth, skipping vendor/cache directories, and emits Resource entries for each VCS-rooted repo and SKILL.md-bearing folder it finds.

nodes.name: computeRepoDrift
nodes.summary: Determines a discovered VCS repo's DriftState by checking its working tree for uncommitted changes via the appropriate VCS backend.

nodes.name: workspace_test.go
nodes.summary: Drives scanWorkspace against synthetic directory trees to confirm depth limits, skip directories, VCS marker detection, and SKILL.md resource emission.

nodes.name: status.go
nodes.summary: Top-level status operation that runs an FS-first discover.Scan and reconciles it with config-declared repos, skills, content, and MCPs to build the StatusReport rendered by the engine.

nodes.name: Collect
nodes.summary: Gathers the current status without side effects: scans the filesystem, asks each manager for its config-driven view, then merges the two into a StatusReport.

nodes.name: Status
nodes.summary: Public entry point that calls Collect and renders the resulting StatusReport to stdout via the requested OutputFormat.

nodes.name: reconcileRepos
nodes.summary: Merges config-declared repo entries (already enriched) with FS-discovered repos, appending any FS-only repos as unmanaged.

nodes.name: reconcileSkills
nodes.summary: Merges config-declared skill entries with FS-discovered skills, filtering out skills that live under known source paths and marking matches as managed.

nodes.name: reconcileMCPs
nodes.summary: Merges config-declared MCP entries with FS-discovered MCP configs, marking matches as managed and appending strangers as unmanaged.

nodes.name: collectRepos
nodes.summary: Projects repo.Manager.Status output into the render-layer RepoEntry shape consumed by formatters.

nodes.name: collectSkills
nodes.summary: Projects skill.Manager.Status output into the render-layer SkillEntry shape consumed by formatters.

nodes.name: collectContent
nodes.summary: Projects content.Manager.Status output into the render-layer ContentEntry shape, including per-target install/missing/modified breakdowns.

nodes.name: collectMCPs
nodes.summary: Projects mcp.Manager.Status output into the render-layer MCPEntry shape consumed by formatters.

nodes.name: collectAgents
nodes.summary: Iterates the agent registry and returns AgentEntry items indicating which agents are installed on the host.

nodes.name: isUnderAny
nodes.summary: Reports whether the given path lies under any of the provided root directories — used to drop skills that originate from a source path the user is intentionally pulling from.

nodes.name: agents_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for the ListAgents and AgentDetail operations covering naming, installed detection, sort order, source field, case-insensitive lookup, and skill counts.

nodes.name: TestListAgents_AllHaveNames
nodes.summary: Confirms ListAgents returns a non-empty name for every entry.

nodes.name: TestListAgents_InstalledDetection
nodes.summary: Confirms ListAgents marks an agent installed when its skill dir exists on disk.

nodes.name: TestListAgents_SortOrder
nodes.summary: Confirms ListAgents returns installed-first then alphabetical order.

nodes.name: TestListAgents_SourceField
nodes.summary: Confirms ListAgents tags each agent as builtin or user based on the registry.

nodes.name: TestAgentDetail_Found
nodes.summary: Confirms AgentDetail returns the requested agent with all paths populated.

nodes.name: TestAgentDetail_CaseInsensitive
nodes.summary: Confirms AgentDetail matches names case-insensitively.

nodes.name: TestAgentDetail_NotFound
nodes.summary: Confirms AgentDetail returns an error for unknown agent names.

nodes.name: TestAgentDetail_SkillCount
nodes.summary: Confirms AgentDetail's reported skill counts match the on-disk SKILL.md files.

nodes.name: audit_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for the Audit operation covering empty-environment output in text and JSON, project vs global skills, two-pass dedupe, and canonical-agent resolution.

nodes.name: makeSkill
nodes.summary: Test helper that creates a SKILL.md file at a relative path inside the workDir.

nodes.name: TestAudit_EmptyEnv_Table
nodes.summary: Confirms Audit produces an empty table when the environment has no skills or MCPs.

nodes.name: TestAudit_EmptyEnv_JSON
nodes.summary: Confirms Audit JSON output is well-formed for an empty environment.

nodes.name: TestAudit_ProjectSkills_Table
nodes.summary: Confirms project-scoped skills appear in the table audit output.

nodes.name: TestAudit_ProjectSkills_JSON
nodes.summary: Confirms project-scoped skills are correctly serialized in the JSON audit output.

nodes.name: TestAudit_TwoPass_NoProjectDuplicate
nodes.summary: Confirms the two-pass audit does not duplicate a project skill that also appears at the global path.

nodes.name: TestAudit_Generic_ProjectSkills
nodes.summary: Confirms generic-convention agents detect project skills via their generic search path.

nodes.name: TestAudit_Generic_GlobalSkills

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Excludes the skills/ directory from the Docker build context since skill fixtures are bind-mounted at runtime, keeping the image small and the build cache stable when only fixtures change.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Two-stage Alpine image for gaal end-to-end tests bundling git/hg/svn, python, node, and the optional agent CLIs (claude-code, codex) on a cacheable base layer with a final stage that overlays the gaal binary.
nodes.languageNotes: Multi-stage build separates a slow cacheable base (apk + npm) from the fast per-PR final layer that only adds the gaal binary; published as ghcr.io/getgaal/gaal-e2e:base-latest for reuse across CI and local dev.

nodes.name: base
nodes.summary: Base stage installing VCS backends (git, mercurial, subversion), Python3, bash/coreutils, ca-certificates, and node/npm on Alpine 3.20.3, with optional agent CLI installation gated on the INSTALL_AGENT_CLIS build arg.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses sleep infinity as CMD so the Go test harness can keep one container alive per `go test` invocation and `docker exec` into it per test.

nodes.name: final
nodes.summary: Final stage copying the pre-built gaal binary into /usr/local/bin and marking it executable; this is the only layer that re-runs per PR build.

nodes.name: ci-selfhosted.yml
nodes.summary: Manual-only CI workflow that runs lint, cross-platform build, race-enabled tests, and coverage on internal self-hosted Linux/macOS-ARM64 runners.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses workflow_dispatch with a `reason` input so triggers are auditable; cache=false because self-hosted runners persist ~/go/pkg/mod across runs.

nodes.name: lint
nodes.summary: Runs gofmt formatting check and go vet via `make lint` on a self-hosted Linux X64 runner.

nodes.name: build
nodes.summary: Compiles the gaal binary on self-hosted Linux and macOS-ARM64 runners via `make build`; depends on the lint job passing.

nodes.name: test
nodes.summary: Runs unit tests with race detector on Linux and macOS-ARM64 via `make test-ci`, uploading JUnit XML test report as an artifact.

nodes.name: coverage
nodes.summary: Generates HTML coverage report via `make coverage-ci` and writes a summary block to the GitHub Step Summary page.

nodes.name: ci.yml
nodes.summary: Primary CI workflow triggered on PR and push-to-main, also exposed as a reusable workflow_call for release.yml; runs lint, doc-change gating, cross-compile build matrix, race-tests, dockerized e2e, and coverage.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a `changes` gating job that diffs against the PR base to skip expensive jobs on docs-only PRs; embeds an inline Python heredoc to parse JUnit XML into a Markdown summary table.

nodes.name: lint
nodes.summary: Runs gofmt formatting check and go vet via `make lint` on ubuntu-latest with Go module caching enabled.

nodes.name: changes
nodes.summary: Detects whether any non-documentation file changed since the PR base or previous push, exposing `code_changed` as an output to gate downstream jobs and skip expensive runners on markdown-only changes.

nodes.name: build
nodes.summary: Cross-compiles every supported target (linux/darwin/windows on amd64+arm64) from the cheapest runners using `make build-cross` with GOOS/GOARCH/CGO_ENABLED=0.

nodes.name: test
nodes.summary: Runs unit tests with race detector on Linux and macOS via `make test-ci`, parses tests.xml with embedded Python to produce a Markdown step-summary table of passed/failed/skipped tests with first-line failure messages.

nodes.name: e2e
nodes.summary: Runs the Layer-1 end-to-end suite — builds gaal for linux/amd64, pre-builds the fixture Docker image with GHA + ghcr.io registry cache, runs `make test-e2e`, posts a JUnit summary, and captures container logs on failure.

nodes.name: coverage
nodes.summary: Generates an HTML coverage report via `make coverage-ci` and posts the text summary to the GitHub Step Summary page; uploads the full report/ directory as an artifact.

nodes.name: install-script.yml
nodes.summary: Path-filtered CI workflow that gates every change to scripts/install.sh and the installscript Go package via shellcheck POSIX mode and a Go integration test against an httptest fake of GitHub Releases.
nodes.languageNotes: Marked as a required status check; pairs with the CODEOWNERS rule on scripts/install.sh so install-pipeline changes always get human review plus automated verification.

nodes.name: shellcheck
nodes.summary: Runs shellcheck in POSIX `sh` mode at `style` severity over the scripts/ directory via ludeeus/action-shellcheck@2.0.0.

nodes.name: integration-test
nodes.summary: Runs `go test -race ./internal/installscript/ -v` on Linux and macOS, exercising the install-script integration tests against a local httptest fake of GitHub Releases.

nodes.name: nightly-e2e-cli.yml
nodes.summary: Scheduled daily (04:17 UTC) Layer-2 e2e workflow that installs the real claude-code and codex CLIs to verify gaal-written configs are accepted by them; too slow for per-PR runs so it runs nightly with manual dispatch.
nodes.languageNotes: Scheduled at an off-peak time (04:17 UTC) to minimize runner contention across US and EU users.

nodes.name: layer2
nodes.summary: Installs gotestsum, runs `make test-e2e-cli` with a 45-minute timeout, uploads the Layer-2 JUnit report, and captures docker inspect/logs on failure.

nodes.name: pr-milestone.yml
nodes.summary: Automation workflow triggered when a merged PR targeting main has no milestone — selects the next open milestone by future due date and assigns it to the PR and any issues referenced via closing keywords in the PR body.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses `gh api` with jq filters to fetch milestones sorted by due_on, then falls back to the most-recent past milestone if all due dates have elapsed.

nodes.name: assign-milestone
nodes.summary: Finds the next open milestone by future due date via the GitHub API, assigns it to the merged PR, then scans the PR body for closing keywords (close/fix/resolve) and assigns the same milestone to any referenced issues that lack one.

nodes.name: publish-e2e-image.yml
nodes.summary: Publishes the test/e2e/fixtures Dockerfile `base` stage to ghcr.io as a long-lived multi-arch registry cache (linux/amd64+arm64) so CI e2e jobs survive GHA cache evictions and local devs can skip slow apk/npm installs.
nodes.languageNotes: Deliberately builds with INSTALL_AGENT_CLIS=0 so frequently-changing Layer-2 agent CLIs don't invalidate the base image cache for every PR.

nodes.name: publish
nodes.summary: Sets up QEMU + buildx, logs in to ghcr.io with GITHUB_TOKEN, builds the `base` target of the fixture Dockerfile for linux/amd64 and linux/arm64, and pushes tags base-latest and base-${sha} with registry cache.

nodes.name: release.yml
nodes.summary: Release workflow triggered on semver tag pushes (v*.*.*) — reuses ci.yml as a precheck, cross-compiles binaries for linux/darwin/windows on amd64+arm64 with rcodesign-based macOS signing/notarization, runs full native test suite on every release platform, then publishes a GitHub Release with checksums.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses rcodesign (apple-platform-rs) instead of Apple's codesign+notarytool to avoid macOS keychain manipulation; signing is gated on semver tag pushes so PR runs cross-compile darwin binaries unsigned for build-regression coverage.

nodes.name: ci
nodes.summary: Reuses .github/workflows/ci.yml via workflow_call to run lint + cross-compile + tests as a release precondition.

nodes.name: binaries
nodes.summary: Cross-compiles gaal release binaries for six target combinations (linux/darwin/windows × amd64/arm64), with conditional rcodesign-based code signing and Apple notarization for darwin targets on tag pushes.

nodes.name: test
nodes.summary: Runs the full test suite natively on every release platform (linux amd64+arm64, macOS, windows) with gotestsum producing JUnit XML, guaranteeing deliverables are 100% validated on their target platforms.

nodes.name: release
nodes.summary: Downloads all gaal-* artifacts, generates SHA256SUMS, and publishes a GitHub Release with auto-generated release notes via softprops/action-gh-release@v2; gated on refs/tags/v so manual dispatches build artifacts without publishing.
