nodes.summary: Technical architecture overview describing the gaal package structure, CLI command flow, internal packages (config, core, discover, engine, repo, skill, mcp, runner, logger), build/test process, and external dependencies.

nodes.name: config.md
nodes.summary: Detailed specification of gaal's five-pillar configuration system covering the YAML data model, multi-level merge strategy, scope-restriction policy with the gaal:"maxscope" tag, JSON Schema generation, platform-specific overrides, and rules for extending the config.

nodes.name: core.md
nodes.summary: Specification of the internal/core package's two pillars: the VCS sub-package (Git and HTTP-archive backends with type detection) and the agent registry sub-package (Info descriptor, built-in agents.yaml, user extension, security constraints).

nodes.name: discover.md
nodes.summary: Guide to the internal/discover package's filesystem-aware drift detection: ResourceType/Scope/DriftState model, snapshot system (FileRecord, hashing, persistence), Scan entry point, and per-resource-type discovery logic for skills, repositories, and MCPs.

nodes.name: quick_start.md
nodes.summary: End-user onboarding walkthrough covering installation, writing a first gaal.yaml, running gaal sync, optional background-service setup, and the status/audit/info commands plus sandbox mode.

nodes.name: status.md
nodes.summary: User-facing reading guide for the gaal status command output, explaining the four screen sections (Repositories, Skills, MCP Configs, Supported Agents), column meanings, drift indicators, and the typical sync/status workflow.

nodes.name: testing.md
nodes.summary: Short testing reference covering end-to-end tests, the cached Docker fixture image, CI artifact collection, and verbose mode for the test harness.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Index page for the gaal command reference, listing all subcommand pages and documenting the conventions (exit codes, flow descriptions, side effects) used throughout the docs/commands/ directory.

nodes.name: agents.md
nodes.summary: Reference documentation for `gaal agents`, describing how the command lists registry-known agents, what "installed" means, exit codes, and the registry source resolution flow.

nodes.name: audit.md
nodes.summary: Reference documentation for `gaal audit`, covering what the command scans across agent configs, drift annotation semantics, exit codes, and the scan flow.

nodes.name: doctor.md
nodes.summary: Reference documentation for `gaal doctor`, detailing the health checks performed (config validity, tooling availability, agent state) and how doctor integrates with the rest of the gaal lifecycle.

nodes.name: info.md
nodes.summary: Reference documentation for `gaal info`, describing environment and configuration introspection output, exit codes, and how `--verbose` adjusts the rendered detail level.

nodes.name: init.md
nodes.summary: Reference documentation for `gaal init`, covering scaffolding of a new gaal.yaml, backup behavior, the documented skeleton output, and the import mode that ingests existing agent configs.

nodes.name: migrate.md
nodes.summary: Reference documentation for `gaal migrate`, explaining the current stub status, its planned role in schema/version upgrades, and exit-code conventions.

nodes.name: schema.md
nodes.summary: Reference documentation for `gaal schema`, describing how the command emits the embedded JSON Schema for gaal.yaml, why it stays in sync with the binary, and IDE setup for YAML validation.

nodes.name: status.md
nodes.summary: Reference documentation for `gaal status`, covering the merge/collect layer, Git-index fast path for drift detection, exit codes, and the end-to-end execution flow.

nodes.name: sync.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive reference for `gaal sync`, the core reconcile command, detailing repo/skill/MCP managers, parallel goroutine execution, atomic install, prune, hooks, service mode, cancellation, and dry-run semantics.
nodes.languageNotes: Most detailed command doc in the project (435 lines, 24 sections); functions as the canonical specification for the sync engine.

nodes.name: version.md
nodes.summary: Reference documentation for `gaal version`, describing version/commit/build-date output, where the values come from (ldflags), and how the User-Agent header propagates to HTTP fetches.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Index page for the per-package documentation set, grouping internal packages into domain, orchestration, and support layers with cross-links to each detail page.

nodes.name: config.md
nodes.summary: Documents the `internal/config` package — its public API, sub-packages, configuration load flow, and recent fixes for the manager that owns gaal.yaml parsing.

nodes.name: content.md
nodes.summary: Documents the `internal/content` package, covering its configuration shape, safety guarantees, and relationship to the skill subsystem.

nodes.name: core-agent.md
nodes.summary: Documents the `internal/core/agent` package, describing the agent registry, initialisation, validateEntry security constraints, and generic agent redirect mechanism.

nodes.name: core-vcs.md
nodes.summary: Documents the `internal/core/vcs` package — its public API, supported backends, archive extraction, subprocess wrappers, and recent URL-mismatch fixes.

nodes.name: discover.md
nodes.summary: Documents the `internal/discover` package, covering the drift heuristic, snapshot lifecycle, state directory layout, and per-resource scanning logic.

nodes.name: engine-ops.md
nodes.summary: Documents the `internal/engine/ops` package — its file layout, reconcile loop, plan-vs-apply semantics, and the backup-on-init mechanism added in PR #198.

nodes.name: engine-render.md
nodes.summary: Documents the `internal/engine/render` package — public renderers, file layout, the pure-function rendering rationale, and sync summary output modes.

nodes.name: engine.md
nodes.summary: Documents the top-level `internal/engine` package — its public API, architecture, service-mode failure cap (PR #201), and cancellation semantics (PR #200).

nodes.name: httpx.md
nodes.summary: Documents the `internal/httpx` HTTP client wrapper — its public API, default settings, request flow, and the rationale behind caller-side body size caps.

nodes.name: installscript.md
nodes.summary: Documents the `internal/installscript` package, explaining its public API, why it lives in `internal`, the bootstrap flow, and its test coverage.

nodes.name: logger.md
nodes.summary: Documents the `internal/logger` package — its public API, output handlers, log flow, and the rationale for using a shared mutex across handlers.

nodes.name: mcp.md
nodes.summary: Documents the `internal/mcp` package covering MCP source modes, codec selection, the order-preserving JSON codec (PR #203), atomic writes, remote fetching, and prune opt-in.

nodes.name: repo.md
nodes.summary: Documents the `internal/repo` package — its public API, clone/update flow, backend selection, parallelism rationale, and the RemoteURLMismatchError and NonEmptyDestinationError contracts.

nodes.name: runner.md
nodes.summary: Documents the `internal/runner` subprocess helper — its public API, log-level-driven behaviour, execution flow, logger coordination, and consumer list.

nodes.name: secfile.md
nodes.summary: Documents the `internal/core/io/secfile` package, explaining why writes are atomic, why mode 0o600 is enforced, and which consumers rely on this primitive.

nodes.name: skill.md
nodes.summary: Documents the `internal/skill` package — source lifecycle, atomic install (PR #204), frontmatter parsing, name validation, file modes, local cache layout, and installation scope.

nodes.name: telemetry.md
nodes.summary: Documents the `internal/telemetry` package — public API, consent model, event flow, on-disk layout, error-redaction rules, and post-run flush rationale.

nodes.name: tools.md
nodes.summary: Documents the `internal/tools` package — its public API, what counts as a declared tool, the discovery flow, consumers, and why tool checks never block sync.

nodes.name: urlx.md
nodes.summary: Documents the `internal/urlx` package — its public API, threat model, validation flow, loopback host detection, URL redaction, and consumer list.

nodes.name: pre-commit
nodes.summary: Git pre-commit hook that runs `make lint` to enforce linter checks before every commit is allowed.

nodes.name: CODEOWNERS
nodes.summary: GitHub CODEOWNERS file requiring project owner review for changes to the high-blast-radius install script.

nodes.name: pull_request_template.md
nodes.summary: GitHub pull request template prompting contributors to describe their change, classify its type, link issues, and complete a review checklist.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore-pattern file (gitignore syntax) listing suggested exclusions for the understand-anything analysis tool, with all rules commented out by default.

nodes.name: agents.yaml
nodes.summary: Built-in agent registry mapping each supported AI agent (amp, claude-code, codex, cline, copilot, etc.) to its skills directory, MCP config location, audit search paths, and platform/capability behaviour flags.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses YAML anchors (&psd, &gas, &vmc, &cps, &cgs) to share path literals across agent entries; the `defaults` and `search_defaults` keys are anchor-only sections ignored by the Go struct decoder.

nodes.name: doc.go
nodes.summary: Package-level doc comment for `installscript`, the Go integration-test package that drives `scripts/install.sh` against a fake GitHub Releases server.

nodes.name: install_test.go
nodes.summary: Integration tests that exercise `scripts/install.sh` against a local `httptest` server impersonating GitHub Releases, covering happy-path install, checksum mismatch, idempotent reinstall, and unsupported-arch failures.

nodes.name: fakeReleaseServer
nodes.summary: Spins up an `httptest.NewServer` that mimics GitHub Releases endpoints (download tarball, SHA256SUMS file, latest tag redirect) so the installer can be exercised offline.

nodes.name: runInstall
nodes.summary: Executes the install script as a subprocess with overridden environment variables and captures combined stdout/stderr for assertions.

nodes.name: detectHostGOOS
nodes.summary: Maps `runtime.GOOS` to the lowercase token the install script expects (`linux`, `darwin`); skips the test on unsupported hosts.

nodes.name: detectHostGOARCH
nodes.summary: Maps `runtime.GOARCH` to the architecture token the install script emits, skipping the test on unsupported hosts.

nodes.name: TestInstallHappyPath
nodes.summary: Verifies the install script downloads the fake binary, validates its checksum, and places the executable at the requested install directory.

nodes.name: TestInstallChecksumMismatch
nodes.summary: Confirms the installer aborts with a checksum error when the SHA256SUMS file lists a value that does not match the downloaded binary.

nodes.name: TestInstallAlreadyInstalled
nodes.summary: Verifies the installer is idempotent: rerunning against an install directory that already contains the target version is a no-op.

nodes.name: TestInstallUnsupportedArch
nodes.summary: Confirms the installer fails clearly when `uname -m` is overridden to report an architecture that has no release asset.

nodes.name: install.sh
nodes.summary: POSIX shell installer for gaal: detects host OS/arch, resolves the requested release version, downloads the tarball and SHA256SUMS from GitHub Releases, verifies the checksum, and unpacks the binary into `INSTALL_DIR`.
nodes.languageNotes: Strict POSIX `sh` (set -eu, no bashisms). Exposes test-only hooks `GAAL_INSTALL_BASE_URL` and `GAAL_INSTALL_ARCH_OVERRIDE` consumed by internal/installscript/install_test.go.

nodes.name: usage
nodes.summary: Prints the script's CLI help text describing recognised environment variables, default install directory, and example invocations.

nodes.name: detect_arch
nodes.summary: Normalises `uname -m` output (or the test-only override) to one of the architecture tokens used in release-asset filenames; exits with an error on unsupported architectures.

nodes.name: resolve_version
nodes.summary: Resolves the `VERSION` env var (default `latest`) by following the GitHub `releases/latest` redirect to obtain the concrete tag.

nodes.name: pick_sha_tool
nodes.summary: Selects an available SHA-256 hashing utility (`sha256sum`, `shasum -a 256`, or `openssl dgst`) for verifying the downloaded asset.

nodes.name: assertions_test.go
nodes.summary: E2E test assertion helpers that verify skill installations, MCP config entries (JSON and TOML), file presence/absence, and dangling symlinks across the per-agent on-disk layout described in agents.yaml.

nodes.name: agentSkillDir
nodes.summary: Resolves an agent's skill directory on disk for the given scope (project/global), expanding `~` against the test HOME.

nodes.name: agentMCPConfigPath
nodes.summary: Resolves an agent's MCP configuration file path for the given scope, returning the expected JSON or TOML location.

nodes.name: AssertSkillInstalled
nodes.summary: Asserts that a named skill is materialised at the expected location for the given agent/scope (presence + SKILL.md).

nodes.name: AssertSkillAbsent
nodes.summary: Asserts that a named skill is NOT present at the expected location for the given agent/scope.

nodes.name: AssertFileExists
nodes.summary: Asserts a path exists inside the test container; fails the test otherwise.

nodes.name: AssertFileAbsent
nodes.summary: Asserts a path does NOT exist inside the test container; fails the test otherwise.

nodes.name: MCPExpect
nodes.summary: Struct describing the expected MCP server entry shape (command, args, env) used to validate the agent's MCP config after sync.

nodes.name: AssertMCPEntry
nodes.summary: Loads the agent's MCP config (JSON or TOML), locates the named server entry, and asserts each field matches the supplied `MCPExpect`.

nodes.name: AssertMCPAbsent
nodes.summary: Asserts that no MCP server with the given name exists in the agent's MCP config (treating an absent config file as success).

nodes.name: AssertNoStaleSymlinks
nodes.summary: Walks a directory and fails the test if any symlink is found whose target does not exist (dangling symlink detection).

nodes.name: decodeJSONServers
nodes.summary: Decodes an agent's JSON MCP config (Claude-style schema with `mcpServers` map) into a normalised slice of server records.

nodes.name: decodeTOMLServers
nodes.summary: Decodes an agent's TOML MCP config (Codex-style schema with `[mcp_servers.NAME]` sections) into a normalised slice of server records.

nodes.name: slicesEqual
nodes.summary: Compares two string slices for equality, used to diff MCP `args` arrays in assertions.

nodes.name: cli_verify_test.go
nodes.summary: Optional E2E layer that drives real agent CLIs (claude, codex) inside the test container to confirm gaal-written configs are accepted by those tools end-to-end. Skipped unless GAAL_E2E_CLI=1.

nodes.name: TestCLI_ClaudeMCPListShowsSyncedServer
nodes.summary: Runs `claude mcp list` against the gaal-managed JSON config and verifies the synced server name appears in its output.

nodes.name: TestCLI_CodexReadsTOMLWritten
nodes.summary: Confirms the codex CLI loads (without error) a TOML config produced by gaal sync.

nodes.name: TestCLI_PruneRemovesEntryFromCLIView
nodes.summary: Verifies that after `gaal prune` removes an MCP entry, the same entry no longer shows up via the agent CLI's listing command.

nodes.name: configs_test.go
nodes.summary: Fluent builder for synthesising gaal config YAML in tests — `configBuilder` exposes AddRepository/AddSkill/AddMCP and renders a quoted, sorted YAML document via String().

nodes.name: configBuilder
nodes.summary: Builder accumulating repository, skill, and MCP entries; rendered to a gaal-compatible YAML config via its `String()` method.

nodes.name: AddRepository
nodes.summary: Appends a repository entry (path, type, url, version) to the builder.

nodes.name: AddSkill
nodes.summary: Appends a skill entry (source, agents, global flag) to the builder.

nodes.name: AddMCP
nodes.summary: Appends an MCP server entry (name, agents, global flag, command, args, env) to the builder.

nodes.name: String
nodes.summary: Renders the accumulated config to a deterministic YAML document with sorted keys, proper indentation, and selective quoting.

nodes.name: quoteIfNeeded
nodes.summary: Quotes a YAML scalar value if it contains characters that would otherwise change parsing semantics.

nodes.name: sortedKeys
nodes.summary: Returns the keys of a `map[string]string` in sorted order so YAML output is deterministic between runs.

nodes.name: container_test.go
nodes.summary: Docker-backed test harness: spawns a long-lived container, exposes `Exec`/`WriteFile`/`ReadFile` primitives, and defines the `testEnv` facade used by every E2E test for isolated HOME directories and gaal invocations.
nodes.languageNotes: Reuses one container for the entire `go test` invocation; per-test isolation is achieved by allocating a fresh HOME via `t.TempDir()` rather than by recycling containers.

nodes.name: TestContainer
nodes.summary: Wrapper around a running Docker container exposing exec, file-IO, and lifecycle primitives shared by every E2E test.

nodes.name: startContainer
nodes.summary: Launches the test container with the fixtures dir bind-mounted read-only and platform-aware Docker arguments, returning a populated `TestContainer`.

nodes.name: Stop
nodes.summary: Stops and removes the test container; safe to call from defers regardless of test outcome.

nodes.name: ExecResult
nodes.summary: Captured outcome of an in-container exec: stdout, stderr, exit code, and combined output convenience.

nodes.name: Exec
nodes.summary: Runs a command inside the container with an explicit env map and working directory, capturing stdout/stderr/exit-code into an `ExecResult`.

nodes.name: MustExec
nodes.summary: `Exec` wrapper that fails the test immediately if the in-container command returns a non-zero exit code.

nodes.name: ExecTTY
nodes.summary: Variant of `Exec` that allocates a PTY in the container so interactive CLIs (e.g. claude, codex) behave as they would on a real terminal.

nodes.name: WriteFileBytes
nodes.summary: Writes a byte payload to a path inside the container by streaming through `docker cp` / `tee`, preserving permissions.

nodes.name: ListDir
nodes.summary: Lists entries of a directory inside the container by running `ls -1` and splitting the output.

nodes.name: testEnv
nodes.summary: Per-test facade bundling a unique HOME directory, the shared TestContainer, and convenience methods for running gaal and writing config files.

nodes.name: newTestEnv
nodes.summary: Allocates a fresh in-container HOME directory for a single test and returns a `testEnv` rooted there.

nodes.name: gaal
nodes.summary: Runs the gaal binary against the supplied config path inside the test env, returning the `ExecResult` for assertion-time inspection.

nodes.name: mustGaal
nodes.summary: `gaal` wrapper that fails the test immediately if gaal returns a non-zero exit code.

nodes.name: toSlice
nodes.summary: Converts the `env map[string]string` test-env API into a `KEY=VALUE` slice for exec invocations.

nodes.name: e2e_test.go
nodes.summary: E2E suite entry point: `TestMain` compiles the gaal binary, builds the Docker image, starts a single shared container, runs every test in the package, and tears down on exit. Gated by the `e2e` build tag.

nodes.name: TestMain
nodes.summary: Suite-wide setup/teardown: ensures the gaal binary and Docker image exist, starts the container, runs `m.Run()`, and stops the container regardless of outcome.

nodes.name: ensureBinary
nodes.summary: Compiles the gaal binary into `test/e2e/fixtures/gaal` for linux/amd64 if it is missing, so the suite can run from an editor without first hitting the Makefile target.

nodes.name: ensureImage
nodes.summary: Builds the `gaal-e2e:dev` Docker image if it is not already cached, reusing layers from prior runs.

nodes.name: repoRoot
nodes.summary: Walks parent directories from the test file up until it finds the repository root (the directory containing `go.mod`).

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Test fixture: SKILL.md describing a realistic multi-file skill, used by sync tests to verify gaal preserves nested skill trees (prompts/, templates/) on install.

nodes.name: main.md
nodes.summary: Test fixture: nested prompts/main.md inside the realistic-skill fixture, used to confirm gaal preserves sub-directories during skill sync.

nodes.name: component.tsx
nodes.summary: Test fixture: tiny React component used by the realistic-skill fixture to verify gaal preserves non-Markdown template files within a skill tree.

nodes.name: Placeholder
nodes.summary:
Trivial React component that renders a `
placeholder
`; exists purely as a sync-target fixture.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Test fixture: second skill SKILL.md, used in tests that verify gaal sync handles multiple skills selected via the `select` filter or wildcards.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Test fixture: minimal stub SKILL.md used by tests that only need a skill to exist on disk, without exercising nested file preservation.

nodes.name: mutate_test.go
nodes.summary: E2E mutation tests verifying that gaal correctly re-syncs after the user changes the config: adding skills/MCPs, swapping scope (project vs global), editing skill content, updating MCP env, and tracking counts in `gaal status`.

nodes.name: TestMutate_AddSkill_PropagatesOnResync
nodes.summary: Adds a new skill to the config and verifies a follow-up `gaal sync` materialises it on disk for every targeted agent.

nodes.name: TestMutate_AddMCP_PropagatesOnResync
nodes.summary: Adds an MCP server to the config and verifies a follow-up sync writes it to every targeted agent's MCP config file.

nodes.name: TestMutate_AddCodexAgent
nodes.summary: Adds codex to the list of targeted agents and verifies a re-sync produces TOML-format MCP config for it without disturbing existing JSON configs.

nodes.name: TestMutate_SwitchScope_PopulatesNewLocation
nodes.summary: Flips a skill from project scope to global scope and verifies the file lands in the new scope's directory after re-sync.

nodes.name: TestMutate_SkillContent_ReSyncUpdates
nodes.summary: Edits the source SKILL.md content and verifies a re-sync overwrites the installed copy with the new content.

nodes.name: TestMutate_MCPEnv_ReSyncUpdates
nodes.summary: Modifies an MCP server's env block in the config and verifies a re-sync overwrites the stored env in every agent's MCP config.

nodes.name: TestMutate_StatusReportsCounts
nodes.summary: Verifies `gaal status` reports accurate counts of skills and MCPs across each agent/scope after a mutation cycle.

nodes.name: negative_test.go
nodes.summary: E2E error-path tests covering malformed YAML, missing config files, unknown skill sources, MCP target collisions, absolute repo paths, and bad VCS version specifiers — confirming gaal rejects them with a useful error.

nodes.name: TestNegative_MalformedYAML
nodes.summary: Confirms gaal exits with a clear parse-error message when handed a syntactically broken YAML config.

nodes.name: TestNegative_MissingConfig
nodes.summary: Confirms gaal exits with a clear error when the requested config path does not exist on disk.

nodes.name: TestNegative_UnknownSkillSource
nodes.summary: Confirms gaal rejects a skill entry whose source path or repository reference cannot be resolved.

nodes.name: TestNegative_MCPTargetIsADirectory
nodes.summary: Confirms gaal fails with a clear message when the path it would write the MCP config to already exists as a directory.

nodes.name: TestNegative_AbsoluteRepoPathRejected
nodes.summary: Confirms gaal rejects repository entries with absolute filesystem paths (the schema requires project-relative paths only).

nodes.name: TestNegative_BadVCSVersionRejected
nodes.summary: Confirms gaal rejects malformed VCS version specifiers (e.g., shell-injection attempts in a git ref string).

nodes.name: prune_test.go
nodes.summary: E2E tests for `gaal prune`: confirms gaal removes installed skills and MCP entries (JSON and TOML) that are no longer referenced by the config, across one or multiple agents.

nodes.name: TestPrune_RemovesOrphanSkill
nodes.summary: Removes a skill from the config and confirms `gaal prune` deletes the previously-synced skill directory from disk.

nodes.name: TestPrune_RemovesOrphanMCPEntry
nodes.summary: Removes an MCP entry from the config and confirms `gaal prune` strips it from the agent's JSON MCP config file.

nodes.name: TestPrune_RemovesOrphanMCPEntry_TOML
nodes.summary: Removes an MCP entry from the config and confirms `gaal prune` strips it from the agent's TOML MCP config (codex case).

nodes.name: TestPrune_AcrossMultipleAgents
nodes.summary: Confirms `gaal prune` removes orphaned entries across multiple agents in a single invocation.

nodes.name: sandbox_test.go
nodes.summary: E2E test verifying gaal honours its sandbox boundary: when given a sandboxed root, every file write must land inside that root and nothing must leak into the real HOME or system paths.

nodes.name: TestSandbox_RedirectsAllWrites
nodes.summary: Runs gaal with a sandboxed install root, then walks HOME and the system paths to assert no file was created outside the sandbox.

nodes.name: scope_test.go
nodes.summary: E2E coverage of the project/global scope matrix: which files land where, dry-run output, audit discovery of previously-installed skills, JSON status shape, and the no-spurious-files invariant.

nodes.name: auditReportShape
nodes.summary: Struct mirroring the JSON shape produced by `gaal audit --json`, used to deserialise the report for inspection in tests.

nodes.name: statusReportShape
nodes.summary: Struct mirroring the JSON shape produced by `gaal status --json`, used to deserialise the report for inspection in tests.

nodes.name: TestScope_Matrix
nodes.summary: Table-driven test sweeping the project/global x skill/MCP matrix, asserting each entry lands at exactly the path implied by agents.yaml.

nodes.name: TestScope_UserConfigLoaded
nodes.summary: Confirms the user-level config (XDG path) is loaded and merged on top of the project config.

nodes.name: TestScope_DryRunReportsChanges
nodes.summary: Verifies `gaal sync --dry-run` lists the pending writes accurately without touching the filesystem.

nodes.name: TestScope_AuditDiscoversInstalledSkill
nodes.summary: Plants a skill outside of gaal's purview and confirms `gaal audit` discovers it via the agents.yaml search paths.

nodes.name: TestScope_StatusJSONShape
nodes.summary: Deserialises `gaal status --json` into `statusReportShape` and asserts every expected field is populated with the right types.

nodes.name: TestScope_NoSpuriousFiles
nodes.summary: After a sync, walks the entire test HOME and asserts only the files declared in the config are present (no leftover scaffolding from previous runs).

nodes.name: smoke_test.go
nodes.summary: Fast smoke tests exercising the gaal CLI surface: `--version`, `schema`, `agents`, `info`, `doctor` (offline + JSON), `init` (empty project + import-all-global), and `migrate --dry-run`.

nodes.name: TestSmoke_Schema
nodes.summary: Runs `gaal schema` and asserts the printed JSON schema document contains the expected top-level keys.

nodes.name: TestSmoke_Agents
nodes.summary: Runs `gaal agents` and asserts the listing includes the canonical built-in agents (claude-code, codex, copilot, etc.).

nodes.name: TestSmoke_DoctorOffline
nodes.summary: Runs `gaal doctor` with networking disabled and asserts the command degrades gracefully rather than crashing.

nodes.name: TestSmoke_DoctorJSON
nodes.summary: Runs `gaal doctor --json` and validates the report deserialises into the expected shape (categories, findings, severity).

nodes.name: TestSmoke_InitEmptyProject
nodes.summary: Runs `gaal init` in an empty directory and asserts a starter config is created with sensible defaults.

nodes.name: TestSmoke_MigrateDryRun
nodes.summary: Runs `gaal migrate --dry-run` against a legacy config and asserts the printed plan describes the expected upgrade steps without modifying anything.

nodes.name: sync_test.go
nodes.summary: Core E2E sync coverage: skills and MCP entries flowing to claude-code (project/global) and codex (global), wildcard agent expansion, nested-tree preservation for realistic skills, and per-file selectors.

nodes.name: preparePresentAgents
nodes.summary: Test-setup helper that pre-creates the directories every targeted agent expects to exist before sync, simulating an already-installed agent layout.

nodes.name: TestSync_SkillToClaudeCode_Project
nodes.summary: Syncs a project-scoped skill to claude-code and asserts the SKILL.md lands at the project's `.claude/skills` directory.

nodes.name: TestSync_SkillToClaudeCode_Global
nodes.summary: Syncs a global-scoped skill to claude-code and asserts the file lands at `~/.claude/skills`.

nodes.name: TestSync_SkillToCodex_Global
nodes.summary: Syncs a global-scoped skill to codex and asserts the file lands at codex's expected global skills directory.

nodes.name: TestSync_MCPToClaudeCode_Global
nodes.summary: Syncs a global MCP server entry to claude-code and asserts the JSON config at `~/.claude.json` contains a matching entry.

nodes.name: TestSync_MCPToClaudeCode_Project
nodes.summary: Syncs a project-scoped MCP server entry to claude-code and asserts the project-level `.mcp.json` carries the entry.

nodes.name: TestSync_MCPToCodex_Global
nodes.summary: Syncs a global MCP server entry to codex and asserts the resulting TOML at `~/.codex/config.toml` matches the expected schema and contents.

nodes.name: TestSync_WildcardAgents_Skills
nodes.summary: Confirms a skill entry whose `agents: '*'` expands to every supported agent that ships an on-disk skills directory.

nodes.name: TestSync_RealisticSkill_PreservesTree
nodes.summary: Syncs the multi-file `realistic-skill` fixture and verifies every nested file (prompts/main.md, templates/component.tsx) is preserved at the target location.

nodes.name: TestSync_SelectFiltersSkills
nodes.summary: Verifies a skill entry with `select:` only materialises the subset of files matched by the selector glob.

nodes.name: tty_test.go
nodes.summary: End-to-end tests verifying gaal's TTY-aware behavior: color output enabled under a pseudo-terminal, disabled without one, and verbose output producing more lines than the default summary.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses pseudo-terminal execution (ExecTTY) and ANSI escape detection (\x1b[) to assert terminal-aware rendering.

nodes.name: TestTTY_ColorEnabledUnderPty
nodes.summary: Asserts that running gaal under a pseudo-terminal with FORCE_COLOR=1 produces ANSI color escape sequences in its output.

nodes.name: TestTTY_ColorDisabledWithoutPty
nodes.summary: Asserts that gaal does not emit ANSI color escape sequences when stdout is not attached to a terminal.

nodes.name: TestTTY_VerboseProducesMoreThanSummary
nodes.summary: Verifies that running gaal with --verbose produces strictly more output lines than the default summary mode for the same configuration.

nodes.name: vcs_git_test.go
nodes.summary: End-to-end tests for the git VCS backend: cloning a bare repository, updating after upstream changes, and pinning to a specific commit version.
nodes.languageNotes: Bootstraps real bare git repositories on disk via shell commands to exercise the full clone/update/pin lifecycle through the gaal CLI.

nodes.name: initBareGitRepo
nodes.summary: Test helper that creates a bare git repository on disk with an initial commit, returning its filesystem path for use as a clone source.

nodes.name: addCommitToBare
nodes.summary: Test helper that adds a new commit to an existing bare git repository by checking out a working clone, writing a file, and pushing back.

nodes.name: TestVCS_GitBackend_CloneAndCheckout
nodes.summary: Verifies gaal can clone a bare git repository and check out its contents at the configured destination path.

nodes.name: TestVCS_GitBackend_UpdateAfterUpstreamChange
nodes.summary: Verifies gaal pulls upstream changes on subsequent runs after a new commit is added to the bare repository.

nodes.name: TestVCS_GitBackend_VersionPin
nodes.summary: Verifies that pinning a repository to an explicit commit hash causes gaal to check out that revision rather than the latest tip.

nodes.name: vcs_other_test.go
nodes.summary: End-to-end tests for non-git VCS backends including Mercurial, SVN, and archive-based (tar.gz / zip) sources, with shared HTTP server and SVN server fixtures.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses sync.Once-style atomic guards (CompareAndSwap) to start shared HTTP and svnserve daemons once per test suite, with poll-based readiness checks.

nodes.name: initBareHgRepo
nodes.summary: Test helper that creates a Mercurial repository on disk with an initial commit for use as a clone source.

nodes.name: TestVCS_HgBackend_CloneAndCheckout
nodes.summary: Verifies gaal can clone a Mercurial repository and check out its contents; skips when the hg binary is unavailable in the test environment.

nodes.name: ensureHTTPServer
nodes.summary: Lazily starts a single shared HTTP file server for the test suite using CompareAndSwap to guarantee one-time startup, then polls until the server accepts connections.

nodes.name: TestVCS_TarBackend_Clone
nodes.summary: Verifies gaal can fetch and extract a tar.gz archive served over HTTP, checking out its contents at the configured destination.

nodes.name: TestVCS_ZipBackend_Clone
nodes.summary: Verifies gaal can fetch and extract a zip archive served over HTTP, checking out its contents at the configured destination.

nodes.name: ensureSvnServer
nodes.summary: Lazily starts a single shared svnserve daemon for the test suite using CompareAndSwap, then polls until the server accepts svn:// connections.

nodes.name: initSvnRepo
nodes.summary: Test helper that creates an SVN repository served by the shared svnserve daemon, with an initial commit containing a sample file.

nodes.name: TestVCS_SvnBackend_CloneAndCheckout
nodes.summary: Verifies gaal can check out an SVN repository served by svnserve; skips when the svn or svnadmin binaries are unavailable.

nodes.name: TestVCS_BzrBackend_Skipped
nodes.summary: Placeholder test that unconditionally skips, marking the Bazaar backend as deliberately untested in this suite.

nodes.name: buildTarGz
nodes.summary: Constructs an in-memory tar.gz archive from a map of filename-to-content entries, used to fabricate archive fixtures for the tar backend test.

nodes.name: buildZip
nodes.summary: Constructs an in-memory zip archive from a map of filename-to-content entries, used to fabricate archive fixtures for the zip backend test.

layers.name: Entry Points & CLI Commands
layers.description: Program entry (main.go) and Cobra subcommand wiring for sync, audit, status, init, agents, doctor, migrate, info, schema, banner, and version, plus the interactive init wizard.

layers.name: Engine Core
layers.description: Sync engine orchestration: per-resource operations (sync, plan, status, audit, agents, doctor, info, init, migrate, telemetry), pre/post hooks, and plan/summary/report renderers in JSON, table, and text.

layers.name: Resource Managers
layers.description: Domain-specific managers that the engine drives: repository sync, SKILL.md installer, MCP server config codec/manager, generic content sync, and drift discovery across global agent directories.

layers.name: Core IO, VCS & Agent Registry
layers.description: Foundational primitives: YAML and secure-file IO, VCS backends (git, hg, svn, bzr, archive) with detection and destination resolution, and the agent registry/behaviour for Claude, Copilot, Cursor, and Codex.

layers.name: Config Loader & Schema
layers.description: YAML config loader with multi-level scope/policy merging, template reflection, platform-specific paths (darwin/linux/windows), and JSON-schema generation/validation via invopop and go-playground/validator.

layers.name: Cross-cutting Utilities
layers.description: Shared infrastructure used across the codebase: pterm logger with redaction and progress, URL normalization/redaction, HTTP client wrapper, command runner, optional PostHog telemetry, tool-existence checks, install-script validation, and the pre-commit hook plus install.sh.

layers.name: End-to-End Tests & Fixtures
layers.description: Container-driven e2e suite covering CLI verification, sync, mutate, prune, scope, sandbox, smoke, tty, VCS, and negative paths, with Dockerfile fixtures and sample skills.

layers.name: Build & CI
layers.description: GitHub Actions workflows (ci, ci-selfhosted, install-script, nightly-e2e-cli, pr-milestone, publish-e2e-image, release), the Makefile build automation, CODEOWNERS, and issue-template config.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: User and contributor docs: README, AGENTS, COMMANDS, PRIVACY_POLICY, the docs/ guide tree (architecture, config, core, discover, commands), GitHub issue templates, PR template, and workflow agent specs.

layers.name: Project Configuration
layers.description: Top-level project manifests and tool config: Go module metadata (go.mod, go.sum), the example gaal.yaml configuration, and the understand-anything ignore file.

tour.title: What gaal Is
tour.description: Start with the README to understand the project's premise: gaal (Governed Agent Access Layer) is a single CLI that reconciles repositories, AI agent skills, and MCP servers across machines from one YAML file. The README spells out the three resource types and the core verbs (sync, audit, status, init) that the rest of the codebase implements. Keep this mental model in mind as the tour walks through how each piece is wired in Go.

tour.title: The YAML Contract
tour.description: Before reading code, look at the input gaal actually consumes. The example configuration shows every supported section — repositories, skills, mcps, content, tools, hooks, telemetry — with the source/agents/global keys that drive the engine's fan-out behaviour. This file is the source of truth that every subsequent step either parses, validates, plans against, or reconciles.

tour.title: Process Entry & Cobra Wiring
tour.description: The Go entry point is intentionally minimal: main.go delegates to cmd.Execute, which boots a Cobra root command. Root configures persistent flags (--config, --verbose, --output, --sandbox, --log-file), loads the YAML chain on every invocation, initialises the pterm-based logger, and threads ExitCodeError for non-zero exits. Every subcommand you'll encounter next hangs off this root.
tour.languageLesson: Cobra builds a tree of *cobra.Command values; each subcommand registers a RunE function that returns an error (rather than panicking), which Cobra propagates to the caller. Go binaries conventionally keep main.go tiny so the cmd/ package owns dispatch.

tour.title: Configuration Loader & Schema
tour.description: internal/config/manager.go is the most-depended-on package in the codebase (fan-in 43): it defines the Config struct, parses scalar-or-list YAML fields, validates against a generated JSON Schema, and merges multi-level scopes (project, user, global) via LoadChain. Schema and policy are siblings that enforce the contract introduced in Step 2 before anything reaches the engine.
tour.languageLesson: Go's struct tags (`yaml:"..."`, `json:"..."`, `jsonschema:"..."`) drive marshalling, validation, and schema generation from a single declaration — a common pattern for keeping data definitions DRY.

tour.title: The Agent Registry
tour.description: agents.yaml is an embedded data file that lists every supported AI agent (claude-code, copilot, cursor, codex, amp, cline, …) and where its skills directory, MCP JSON config, and audit paths live on each OS. The registry loader reads this file plus any user override and exposes Lookup/Names/List — the lens every resource manager uses to translate a logical agent name into concrete filesystem paths.

tour.title: The Engine Facade
tour.description: internal/engine/engine.go (fan-out 124, the broadest in the codebase) wires the four resource managers (repo, skill, content, mcp) plus hooks behind one struct and exposes every public verb the cmd layer calls — RunOnce, Plan, Status, Audit, Info, Init, Doctor. Each command in cmd/ constructs an Engine with options and calls a single method; the engine in turn delegates to the ops/ package for the actual business logic.

tour.title: Resource Managers — Repos, Skills, MCPs, Content
tour.description: These four managers are the heart of the reconciler. Repo manager dispatches each declared source to the right VCS backend in parallel; skill manager scans SKILL.md collections and installs them into per-agent directories; MCP manager merges server entries into agent JSON without clobbering user config; content manager copies arbitrary paths into agent skill roots with atomic replacement. Each writes a post-sync snapshot used later for drift detection.

tour.title: VCS Backends
tour.description: The repo manager from Step 7 hides a small abstraction: a VCS interface (Clone/Update/IsCloned/CurrentVersion/HasChanges) with backends for git (go-git), hg, svn, bzr, and tar/zip archives. detect.go infers the right backend from local markers or URL suffix; destination.go centralises the refuses-to-clone-into-non-empty-dir guard that every backend shares. This is also where the README's multi-protocol repos claim becomes real code.
tour.languageLesson: Compile-time interface assertions like `var _ VCS = (*Git)(nil)` make Go fail to build if a type stops satisfying an interface — a lightweight way to guarantee polymorphism without runtime surprises.

tour.title: Plan, Status, Audit Operations
tour.description: The engine doesn't perform writes directly — it calls into internal/engine/ops/. plan.go classifies every configured resource into clone/create/update/no-op/error without touching the filesystem (this is what powers --dry-run). status.go runs a filesystem-first scan and reconciles it with the YAML to show drift. audit.go discovers everything installed on disk that is NOT in the YAML, helping users author their config from an existing setup. info.go and doctor.go round out the read-only surface.

tour.title: Hooks, Renderers & Output
tour.description: Before and after a sync the engine runs user-defined pre/post hooks (with timeout, env expansion, OS filtering). After every operation, results flow through the Renderer interface, which has JSON, table (pterm), and text implementations selected by --output. Renderer is the second-highest fan-in subsystem after config — every command's results pass through it, which is why all summary_*.go variants are kept side by side.

tour.title: The Init Wizard
tour.description: gaal init is the on-ramp the README highlights: it bootstraps gaal.yaml from whatever is already installed. cmd/init.go orchestrates the TTY prompts (scope picker, multi-select of skills and MCP servers) while init_build.go runs the audit discovery passes that feed those prompts. Together they close the loop — Step 9's audit discovers, the wizard curates, and the result becomes the YAML from Step 2.

tour.title: Build, CI & End-to-End Tests
tour.description: The Makefile is the canonical build entry (build, test-race, lint, cross-compile, sandboxed run targets); ci.yml chains those targets on every PR plus a dockerized e2e matrix. The e2e Dockerfile bakes git/hg/svn, node, python, and the optional agent CLIs (claude-code, codex) into a two-stage image so tests exercise real reconciliation against real binaries — the strongest evidence that the architecture from Steps 3-11 actually delivers what the README promises.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage Dockerfiles share a cacheable `base` layer (slow-changing OS packages and language runtimes) and a thin `final` layer that overlays the freshly-built binary, so e2e iterations rebuild in seconds rather than minutes.
